Name: Abandoned Willow
Card Text: Immediately remove 1 empty field from your farmyard and receive 4 Wood. (That space now counts as unused).

Name: Absinthe
Card Text: Place 1 Food on the next 5 remaining Round spaces. At the start of these rounds’ Work phase, place the Food on an Action space of your choice. (The next player to use this space receives the Food.)

Name: Academic
Card Text: This card counts as 2 occupations for Minor Improvements and when scoring the 'Reeve' occupation card.

Name: Acater
Card Text: Whenever you place Food from the supply on a future Round space you may place 1 additional Food per space.

Name: Acquirer
Card Text: At the start of each round, you may pay Food equal to the number of Family members you have to buy 1 Good of your choice from the supply.

Name: Acreage
Card Text: When you sow, you can plant grain on this card. There can be up to 2 stacks of grain on this card, as shown.

Name: Acrobat
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Travelling Players' action on an Action Space, after all other players have had their turns you may move that person to a free 'Plough' or 'Take 1 Grain' Action space and take the action.

Name: Adoptive Parents
Card Text: When you take the 'Family Growth' action, you can pay 1 food and place the offspring on an Action space in the same round. The offspring does not then count as 'newborn'.

Name: Agrarian
Card Text: At the start of each round, you may place 1 Food from your supply on the ”Plow 1 field” Action space. Any player that uses that space also receives the Food. At the end of the round, if the space has not been used, you take the Food back and may Plow 1 field.

Name: Alchemist’s Lab
Card Text: This card is an additional Action space for all players. Any player who uses this Action space must pay you 1 Food to receive 1 building resource of every type that they already have in their supply.

Name: Allegory
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 1 Sheep, 1 Wild Boar and 1 Cattle.

Name: Almanac
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately take the field and breeding phases of a harvest on your farmyard. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Alms
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 1 food for each completed round of the game. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Alms Basket
Card Text: When you play this card, take 1 Grain for every 2 completed rounds of the game.

Name: Alms Collector
Card Text: When you play this card, if it is your first Occupation, you receive 1 Food for every other Occupation in play (by any player) up to a maximum 7 Food.

Name: Almshouse
Card Text: At the end of the feeding phase of each Harvest, you may discard 1 Begging card that you received in any Harvest because you paid 1 Food less to feed your family.

Name: Alternative Designer
Card Text: Whenever you build stables or fences, you may replace wood with the same amount of clay or stone. Whenever you build rooms, you may replace wood with the same amount of clay or stone, to a maximum of 2 clay and 2 stone.

Name: Alternative Materials
Card Text: Once during the Feeding phase of each Harvest you may convert 1 Wood to 2 Food and/or 1 Stone to 2 Food.

Name: Amazon
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Fishing” Action space in Round 7/9/11/13, you receive 1 additional Sheep/Wild boar/Cattle animal of your choice.

Name: Amusement Park
Card Text: Place 1 Food each on the next 2/3/4/5 remaining Round spaces if you have 1/2/3/4 pastures. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.

Name: Animal Breeder
Card Text: When you fence unused spaces to create at least one new pasture, you can buy a pair of animals: 2 sheep for 1 food, 2 wild boar for 2 food or 2 cattle for 3 food.

Name: Animal Buyer
Card Text: At the end of the Field phase of each Harvest, you may buy 1 or 2 animals. Pay 2/3/4 Food for each Sheep/Wild boar/Cattle.

Name: Animal Dealer
Card Text: Whenever you use one of the 'Sheep', 'Wild boar' or 'Cattle' Action spaces, you can pay 1 food to take 1 additional animal of that type.

Name: Animal Farm
Card Text: Once per round, you may return 1 of your animals to the supply for a guest token, which you place this round as you would a family member. (The guest represents the animal.)

Name: Animal Farmer
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each of your pastures that cannot contain any more animals than it does. If all of your pastures qualify, you receive 1 additional bonus point.

Name: Animal Feed
Card Text: Immediately before scoring, you receive 1 additional animal of each type that you already have.

Name: Animal Handler
Card Text: Place 1 sheep on the space for Round 7, 1 wild boar on Round 10 and 1 cattle on Round 14. At the start of these Rounds, you can buy the animal for 1 food.

Name: Animal Holder
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 wood. At the end of the game, each player who has the most animals receives 3 bonus points.

Name: Animal Keeper
Card Text: You can keep sheep, wild boar and cattle in the same pasture. This applies to all your pastures.

Name: Animal Nursery
Card Text: You may place newborn animals of any type on this card immediately after their birth. They cannot be used for any purpose or moved of this card, but are counted at the end of the game. (This card does not count as a pasture or stable.)

Name: Animal Pen
Card Text: Place 2 food on each remaining Round space. At the start of each round, you receive the food.

Name: Animal Renovator
Card Text: This card can hold 2/4 Animals of your choice if you live in a Clay Hut/Stone house. When you play this card, you may renovate your Wooden Hut.

Name: Animal Tamer
Card Text: You can keep 1 animal in each room of your home. You may keep more than 1 type of animal in your home.

Name: Animal Trainer
Card Text: Whenever you take food from a ”Traveling players” space, you may immediately use it to buy animals: Pay 2 food for each sheep or wild boar and 3 food for each cattle.

Name: Animal Trap
Card Text: Place 1 cattle, 1 grain, and 1 cattle on the next 3 round spaces (in that order). At the start of these rounds, you receive the good.

Name: Animal Welfarist
Card Text: At the end of each Work phase in which you used both the ”Build Stables” action on an Action space and the ”Build Fences” action on an Action space, you receive 1 animal of each kind.

Name: Animal Yard
Card Text: Place up to two animals of your choice on this card. They need not be the same type of animal.

Name: Apiary
Card Text: At the end of each Work phase, you may Sow 1 field.

Name: Apple Garden
Card Text: You receive 2 Bonus points for either Grain or Vegetables missing from your farm at the end of the game. (The fruit replaces this type of good.) (You still lose the points for the missing good.)

Name: Apple Picker
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Take 1 Grain” or ”Take 1 Vegetable” Action space, you receive an additional 1 Wood and 1 Food.

Name: Apple Tree
Card Text: Place 1 Wood from the general supply on an empty field. This field still counts as a field, but can no longer be used for sowing or other uses. In the field phase of each Harvest, you receive 1 Food.

Name: Appraiser
Card Text: Whenever you play an Improvement with at least 2 different Building resources in the printed cost, you receive 1 Wood and 1 Food before paying the costs of the Improvement.

Name: Apprentice
Card Text: At the beginning of every harvest, you receive 1 food for every major improvement you own worth at least 2 points without counting bonus points.

Name: Apprenticeship
Card Text: Immediately play an Occupation if you have fewer Occupations than each other player. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Aquavit
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 4 Food and 1 Begging card. At any time, you may pay 3 Food to discard that Begging card.

Name: Archangel
Card Text: Place 2 Reed on the ”Take 1 Grain” Action space from the general supply. Any player who uses that space may also receive 1 Reed from it. Once there is no Reed on that space, you receive 1 Vegetable.

Name: Archer
Card Text: Whenever you receive animals from an Action space, immediately afterward you receive 1/2/3 Wood if you have 1/2/3+ people on Action spaces that provide animals.

Name: Architect
Card Text: Once during the game, at any time after your home reaches at least 5 rooms, you may extend it by 1 room at no cost.

Name: Art
Card Text: (This card has no effect.)

Name: Art Director
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Traveling Players” Action, you receive an additional 1 Food and a building resource of your choice.

Name: Artichoke field
Card Text: When you Sow, you may Sow on this card as if it were 1 field. Whenever you harvest 1 or more goods from this card during the Field phase of Harvest, you receive 1 Food.

Name: Artisan
Card Text: At most once per harvest, when you use an improvement to convert a building resource to food, you receive 2 additional food.

Name: Artists Garden
Card Text: In the Returning home phase of each round, you may harvest 1 field.

Name: Astronomer
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 2 food. Whenever 3/4/5 of your family members (or guests) use 3/4/5 adjacent action spaces (forming a vertical or horizontal line), you receive 1/3/5 bonus points. The action spaces must have the same dimensions size.

Name: Attic
Card Text: Whenever you extend your home, you may place 1 Wood from your supply on each of the new rooms. You cannot use this wood any more, but each is worth 1 Bonus point at the end of the game.

Name: Auctioneer
Card Text: Place 2 reed, 3 wood, 3 clay, and 2 stone on rounds 7, 9, 11 and 13, respectively. At the start of these rounds, all players simultaneously bid food for the resources. The highest bidder gets the resources and you get the food. If you win, pay your food to the general supply. Ties are broken by this round’s player order.

Name: Award Winner
Card Text: Once per round, when you build a Major or Minor Improvement, you may pay 1 additional building resource of those already paid for 1 Bonus Point.

Name: Axe
Card Text: Whenever you add a room to your Wooden hut, you only pay 2 wood and 2 reeds.

Name: Baby Sling
Card Text: Once per round when you take the ”Family growth” action on an Action space, you can move the newborn(s) to Baby Sling. If you do, return the adult who took ”Family growth” to your home.

Name: Back Door
Card Text: Immediately place a Family member that is not already placed on an Action space on the ”After Family Growth also 1 Minor Improvement” Action space even if it is occupied by you or another player and take that action. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Backyard Well
Card Text: Once this game, at any time, you may place 1 Stone from the general supply on 1 Unused farmyard space next to your home. (It now counts as used.) If you do this, place 1 Food on each of the next 3 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food. (Backyard Well counts as a Well.)

Name: Badger
Card Text: Immediately place a marker on an Action space of your choice. At the start of each round, you must move it to an orthogonally adjacent revealed Action space. Any player that uses that space also receives 1 Food.

Name: Baguette
Card Text: During each Harvest, you may pay 1 Wood to Bake bread at the start of the Feeding phase if you have an Improvement with the bread symbol.

Name: Bakehouse
Card Text: Whenever you use a bread baking action, you can use the Bakehouse to convert up to 2 grain into 5 food each. When you play this card, you can also bake bread immediately.

Name: Baker
Card Text: During each Harvest, you may Bake bread at the start of the Feeding phase if you have a Baking improvement. When you play this card, you may Bake bread as an additional action.

Name: Baker’s Daughter
Card Text: If another player plays the Baker E150 or Master Baker E149, you may play this card immediately at no cost. You can use the ”Take 1 Grain” and ”Sow and Bake Bread” action spaces even if another player has placed a person on the space. When you play this card, you can immediately take a ”Bake bread” bread baking action.

Name: Baker’s Kitchen
Card Text: Whenever you use a bread baking action, you can use the Baker’s Kitchen to convert up to 2 grain into 5 food each. When you play this card, you can also bake bread immediately .

Name: Baker’s Oven
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Bake bread' action, you can use the Baker's Oven to convert up to 2 grain into 5 food each. When you play this card, you can also take the 'Bake bread' action.

Name: Baker’s servant
Card Text: Whenever you use a ”Bake bread” action, you may Plow 1 field.

Name: Bakery
Card Text: You receive 4 Food at the start of each Harvest.

Name: Baking Field
Card Text: When you sow, you can plant grain on this card as you would on a field. When harvesting, you must immediately bake this field’s harvested grain.

Name: Baking Tray
Card Text: Clay Ovens and Stone Ovens are Minor improvements for you. Clay, Stone and Wooden ovens cost you 1 resource (of your choice) less.

Name: Balance Farmer
Card Text: At the start of each round, if you have 2/3/4 types of building resources with the same amount (except 0), you receive 1/2/3 Food.

Name: Bamboo
Card Text: Your next 5 fences cost you nothing.

Name: Banana Tree
Card Text: Place 1 Food each on the next 7 remaining Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.

Name: Banana Worker
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 1 Wood and 1 Clay. Then you may play a Minor Improvement card by paying its costs.

Name: Banker
Card Text: Immediately place a marker on 1 Action space of your choice. At the start of each round, move the marker to an orthogonally adjacent Action space. Whenever anyone uses that Action space that provides Goods with the marker on it, they receive 1 additional Good provided.

Name: Barbecue Man
Card Text: At any time, you may pay 1 Wood to gain the ability to convert animals and Vegetables to Food for the rest of the round. Take 2 Food for each Sheep, 3 Food for each Wild boar or Vegetable, and 4 Food for each Cattle.

Name: Barber shop
Card Text: For every Harvest remaining when you play this card, you receive 1 Bonus point.

Name: Bard
Card Text: Whenever another player uses the ”Traveling players” action on an action space, you receive 1 food and 1 wood from the general supply.

Name: Bargain Hunter
Card Text: At the start of each round, you may place 1 Food from your supply on the ”Traveling Players” Action space to play a Minor Improvement. (The next player to use the Action space receives the Food.)

Name: Bargaining Baker
Card Text: When you build an Oven, pay 1 Building resource less of your choice. Whenever you build a ”baking symbol” Improvement, you receive 1 Grain before building the Improvement. (An Oven is a ”baking symbol”-Improvement with ”Oven” in the name.)

Name: Bargaining Harvester
Card Text: Whenever you harvest 1/2/3/4+ Goods you receive 1 Food/1 Sheep/1 Wild Boar/1 Animal of your choice.

Name: Bark Breads
Card Text: When you play this card you receive 2 Food. In addition, you may convert up to 2 Wood into 1 Food each. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Barn
Card Text: At the start of Rounds 8, 10, 12, and 14, you may pay 1 Wood to builds stable if you have more fields than stables.

Name: Barn Master
Card Text: When you play this card, you can build 1 Stable at no cost in each of your pastures that can not contain any more animals than it does.

Name: Barrel Maker
Card Text: Whenever you have at least 7 Wood at the start of a harvest, you receive 1 Wood and 2 Food.

Name: Bartering Hut
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately buy up to 2 animals from the general supply. Pay any 2 building resources for each sheep, any 3 for each wild boar, and any 4 for each cattle.

Name: Bartering Shack
Card Text: When you play this card, convert 1 Sheep/1 Wild Boar/1 Cattle into 2/3/4 Building resources of your choice. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Basin Maker
Card Text: For each wild boar that you convert into food, you can place up to 2 wood from your personal supply on this card. You receive 1 bonus point for each wood on this card except the 1st, 4th, 7th and 10th.

Name: Basket
Card Text: Whenever you use a person's action to take wood, you can leave 2 of the wood on the Action space and receive 3 food in exchange.

Name: Basketmaker
Card Text: In each harvest, the Basketmaker can convert up to 1 reed to 3 food.

Name: Basket Weaver
Card Text: In each Harvest, the Basket Weaver can convert up to 1 reeds to 3 food.

Name: Bathroom
Card Text: Each time you convert a Grain or Vegetable into Food, you may place it on this card. For each field you Sow, you may take 1 Good of the same type from this card and add it to that field. (Goods on this card do not count for points when scoring.)

Name: Bean Field
Card Text: When Sowing, you can plant vegetables on this card as though it were a field.

Name: Bean Hole
Card Text: You may use the Bean Hole to convert Vegetables to 5 Food each. Place the Food on the next Rounds action space. At the start of that Round you receive the Food.

Name: Beastmaster
Card Text: You may no longer convert animals to Food. Whenever you use an animal Accumulation space, you receive an additional 3 Food. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each animal category in which you score the maximum 4 points.

Name: Beauty and the Beast
Card Text: Whenever your second Family member uses an Action space orthogonally adjacent to the Action space occupied by your first Family member, you receive 2 Food.

Name: Beehive
Card Text: Place 2 food on each remaining even-numbered round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

Name: Beehive Oven
Card Text: Whenever you use a bread baking action, you can use the Beehive Oven to convert up to 1 grain into 4 food. Whenever you bake 1 or more grain into bread without using the Beehive Oven, you receive 1 additional food. When you play this card, you can also bake bread immediately.

Name: Bee Keeper
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately build 1 stable without paying Wood and place it on its side. The stable loses its regular animal functions. (It is still worth 1 additional point if fenced.) In the Feeding phase of each harvest, you receive 1 Food as long as the stable is on your farm.

Name: Benefactor
Card Text: Once during the game, when you have 4 fenced pastures, you may return 1 animal to the supply to extend your Wooden hut by 1 room at no cost.

Name: Berry Picker
Card Text: Whenever you use a family member's action to take wood, take an additional 1 food.

Name: Birdcage
Card Text: Each Harvest, you may place 1 Food on this card from your supply. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each food on this card. You may not hold Sheep, Wild Boar, or Cattle in your home. (Even if another card allows this).

Name: Birthstone
Card Text: Whenever you grow your family, you may pay 1 stone for 1 bonus point. When you play Birthstone, you may immediately do this for any new family member that you already received this round.

Name: Birth Well
Card Text: If you play this card in Round 5 or after, you may immediately take a ”Family Growth” action even without room. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Blackberry Farmer
Card Text: Whenever you build fences, place Food on each remaining round space, up to the number of fences just built. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.

Name: Blacksmith
Card Text: Whenever any player plays an Improvement, they may pay 1 less Stone and pay 1 more Food instead. Whenever another player does this, you receive 1 Food. When you play this card, you may play an Improvement with a printed Stone cost.

Name: Black Swan Lake
Card Text: Place 1 Food on each of the next Round spaces, up to the number of your played Occupations. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.

Name: Boar Breeding
Card Text: When you play this card,you receive 1 wild boar. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Boarding School
Card Text: The next time you use ”Family growth”, you may add 3 to the current round and place your newborn on the corresponding Round space. If you do, at the start of that round, place the offspring as an adult in your home even without room and play up to 2 Occupations for free.

Name: Boar Lord
Card Text: At the start of each Harvest, if you are the only player with most Wild Boar, you may Plow 1 field.

Name: Boar Trap
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Take 1 Grain” Action space, you receive 1 Wild Boar.

Name: Boat Constructor
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Fishing” Action space you receive 1 additional Food and 1 Wood.

Name: Boatswain
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Fishing” Action space, you may place 1 Grain from the supply on each of your empty plowed fields. These fields are now considered planted fields. (This does not count as a ”Sow” action)

Name: Bonanza
Card Text: Whenever you use a ”Take Stone” accumulation space, you also receive 1 Grain.

Name: Bone Collector
Card Text: For each Wild Boar, Cattle, or 2 Sheep you convert into Food, you receive 1 Wood from the general supply.

Name: Bonfire
Card Text: Place 1 Wood from the general supply on 1 Unused farmyard space. As long as the Wood remains, the space counts as used. You may return it at any time to convert animals to Food for the rest of the round. The conversion rates are: sheep →2 food; wild boar →3 food; cattle →4 food.

Name: Bonus
Card Text: (This card has no effect.)

Name: Bonus Room
Card Text: This card provides room for 1 Family member.

Name: Bookmark
Card Text: Add 3 to the current round and place a marker on the corresponding Round space. At the start of that round, you may play 1 Occupation for free.

Name: Bookshelf
Card Text: Whenever you play 1 occupation, you receive 3 food before you pay the costs of the occupation.

Name: Borrowed Plow
Card Text: Immediately plow 2 Fields and pay 1 Wood to the player on your right. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Borrower
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 7 Food and 2 Begging cards. At any time, you may pay 3 Food to discard one of these 2 Begging cards or a Begging card you received instead of paying 1 Food to feed your family.

Name: Botanist
Card Text: Whenever you use a Stone Accumulation space, you receive 1 additional Food and 1 Grain or 1 Vegetable.

Name: Boundary Stones
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3/5 Bonus points for having at least 2/3/4/5 fields that are orthogonally adjacent to exactly one other field.

Name: Bountiful Harvest
Card Text: If you harvest at least 2 resources and breed at least 1 animal during a harvest, place 1 food from the general supply on this card. You may use this food at any time. At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each food on this card.

Name: Bounty
Card Text: Whenever you play a card with a Bonus point symbol, you may pay 1 Food to receive a Vegetable.

Name: Bowyer
Card Text: Whenever another player takes Sheep/Boar/Cattle from an action space, you receive 1 Food/1 Wood/1 Stone.

Name: Braggart
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1/3/5/7/9 Bonus points for having 5/6/7/8/9 improvements in front of you.

Name: Braid Maker
Card Text: For you, the Basket Maker’s Workshop is a Minor Improvement and costs only 1 Reed and 1 Stone. In each Harvest, the Braid Maker can convert up to 1 Reed to 2 Food.

Name: Branch Collector
Card Text: Whenever you use Clay or Stone for an Extension, Renovation, or Improvement, you receive 1 Wood from the general supply before paying its costs.

Name: Brazier
Card Text: During the Feeding phase of each Harvest, you can use the Brazier to convert exactly 1 animal or Vegetable to Food as follows: vegetable →3 food; sheep→2 food; wild boar →3 food; cattle →4 food.

Name: Bread Carrier
Card Text: Once any player has built an Oven, place 1 Food on the Remaining round spaces. At the start of the rounds, you receive the Food. (If an Oven has already been built, place the Food immediately. If multiple Ovens are built, you only receive the Food once.)

Name: Bread Machine
Card Text: Once each round, you may skip placing a Family member and receive a ”Bake bread” action instead. You place the person later. You may only do this if every player has at least 1 person still to place.

Name: Bread Paddle
Card Text: Whenever you play an occupation, you may also bake bread.

Name: Bread Seller
Card Text: Take 1 food from the supply for each bread that is baked whenever any player (including you) bakes bread.

Name: Breakfast Outdoors
Card Text: In each Feeding phase, you receive 1 Food.

Name: Brewery
Card Text: During the feeding phase of the harvest, you can use the Brewery to convert at most 1 grain to 3 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for your ninth grain.

Name: Bricklayer
Card Text: Each improvement and renovation costs you 1 clay less. Each room costs you 2 clay less.

Name: Brickyard
Card Text: At the start of each round, if you have no Clay in your supply, place 1 Clay on this card. At any time when this card has 4 or more Clay on it, you may move all of the Clay to your personal supply.

Name: Broom
Card Text: Discard all the remaining minor improvements in your hand, and draw 7 new minor improvements. You can play 1 more minor improvement immediately.

Name: Brush Maker
Card Text: Whenever you convert wild boar to food, place them on this card. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 bonus points for 2/3/4 slaughtered wild boar.

Name: Brushwood Collector
Card Text: You may replace the required reeds with 1 wood for any renovation or extension.

Name: Brushwood Roof
Card Text: Whenever you extend or renovate your home, you can replace 1 or 2 reed with the same amount of wood.

Name: Builder’s Trowel
Card Text: You can renovate your Wooden hut to a Clay hut at any time, without using the 'Renovate' action. (You must still pay for the Renovation).

Name: Building Material
Card Text: Take either 1 wood or 1 clay when you play this card. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left.

Name: Building Planner
Card Text: Place 1 Stable on the space for round 6, 4 Fences on Round 8 and 1 Wooden Room on Round 10. At the start of these rounds, you can build each item and pay 1 Wood less.

Name: Burger Flipper
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Traveling Players” or ”Fishing” Action space, you may move exactly 1 Food from that space to the other space and receive 1 Cattle.

Name: Buried Treasure
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Plow 1 field” or ”Plow 1 field and/or Sow” Action space, you also receive 1 Stone.

Name: Businessman
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Starting player' Action space, you can play an additional Minor Improvement or a Major Improvement after you play the Minor Improvement.

Name: Business Tutor
Card Text: Whenever you renovate your Wooden hut, you may play up to 2 Improvements by paying their costs. Whenever you renovate your Clay hut, you may pay 1 Food to play up to 2 Occupations.

Name: Businesswoman
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately play up to 2 improvements (any combination of major and minor) by paying their costs.

Name: Butcher
Card Text: You can change your animals into food at any time. Take 1 food for each sheep, 2 for each wild boar and 3 for each cattle.

Name: Butter Churn
Card Text: Whenever you have sheep during the Field phase of a Harvest, you receive 1 food for each third sheep. Whenever you have cattle during the Field phase, you receive 1 food for each second cattle.

Name: Cabbage Lover
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 1 Vegetable. For each Vegetable that you convert into Food, you can place 1 Clay from your personal supply on this card. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each Clay on this card, up to a maximum of 5 points.

Name: Cabinetmaker
Card Text: In each Harvest, the Cabinetmaker can convert up to 1 wood to 2 food.

Name: Cabinet Owner
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you receive 1/2/3/4 Wood. At the end of the game, players who have the most played Improvements with a printed Wood cost receive 3 Bonus points each.

Name: Cactus Lover
Card Text: When you play this card, for each built stable in your farm, you may immediately play 1 Major or Minor Improvement by paying its costs.

Name: Camembert
Card Text: Place 1 Food each on 3 Action spaces (unoccupied or occupied) from the general supply. The next player to use the Action space receives the Food.

Name: Candy Room
Card Text: Whenever you receive Food from an Action space, you receive 1 Bonus Point.

Name: Canoe
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Catch Fish' Action space, you receive an additional 1 food and 1 reeds.

Name: Caravan
Card Text: This card provides room for 1 Family member.

Name: Card Player
Card Text: From bottom to top, stack 1 Stone, Reed, Clay, and Wood on this card. At the end of each round, move the top resource to the bottom of the stack. Whenever you receive the top/bottom resource type, take 1 additional/fewer resource of that type. (Take from or move the resource to the supply).

Name: Carpenter
Card Text: To extend your home, you need only 3 of the appropriate resource and 2 reeds.

Name: Carpet
Card Text: You can use any ”Family growth” Action space, even if it is occupied by another player.

Name: Carp Pond
Card Text: Place 1 food on each remaining odd-numbered Round space. At the start of these rounds, take the food.

Name: Carrot Cake Baker
Card Text: Whenever you convert vegetables into food, you may additionally take the ”Bake bread” a bread baking action. The Carrot Cake Baker can also convert 1 vegetable into 2 food at any time.

Name: Carrot Farmer
Card Text: Place 3 vegetables from the general supply on this card. Whenever you use the ”Plough 1 Field” action space, you may pay 1 food for one of the vegetables.

Name: Carrot Harvester
Card Text: Whenever you harvest 2 or more Vegetables, you receive 1 Bonus point.

Name: Carrots
Card Text: Place 1 Food on each remaining even-numbered Round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.

Name: Cash Crop
Card Text: When you sow, you may sow on this card as if it were 1 field. Whenever you harvest goods from this card during the Field phase of a Harvest, you must exchange these harvested goods for 1 Bonus point each.

Name: Casino Manager
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 Wood. At the end of the game, all players with the fewest remaining playing pieces (Family members, fences, and stables) in their supply receive 3 Bonus points each.

Name: Cat Lover
Card Text: If there are 1/2/4/7 animals on your farm, you may pay 1/2/3/4 fewer building resources of your choice for each new Wooden room.

Name: Cattle Breeder
Card Text: Your cattle also breed at the end of Round 12, if there is place for the calf. When you play this card, you receive 1 cattle.

Name: Cattle Dealer
Card Text: Whenever you fence a new pasture that covers at least 3 farmyard spaces, you can pay 1 Reed to receive 2 Cattle.

Name: Cattle Market
Card Text: When you play this card, take 1 cattle. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left.

Name: Cattle Rancher
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Day Laborer” Action space, you may pay 1 Food for 1 Cattle. From Round 6, this costs you nothing.

Name: Cattle Whisperer
Card Text: Add 5 and 9 to the current round. Place 1 cattle on the corresponding Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, take the cattle.

Name: Ceramics
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 2 food. From now, the Pottery is a Minor Improvement for you and costs you nothing.

Name: Chamberlain
Card Text: From Round 11, you (and only you) can use the Actions on the Round cards for the following rounds.

Name: Chameleon
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 1 Wild boar. You may keep 1 Wild boar in each of your pastures that hold Sheep. (Pastures can still only hold the normal amount of animals).

Name: Champion Breeder
Card Text: Whenever you place 2/3 newborn animals on your farm during the Breeding phase of the Harvest, you receive 1/2 Bonus points.

Name: Changing Plans
Card Text: Immediately return 1 or 2 stables from your farmyard to your supply. Afterward, Plow 1 field for every stable returned (in the same or different space). (Normal plowing rules still apply.) After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Charcoal Burner
Card Text: Take 1 food and 1 wood whenever any player (including you) builds a Fireplace, Cooking Hearth, Cooking Corner, Baker's Kitchen, Bakehouse or Oven.

Name: Cheapskate
Card Text: When you play this card, if it is your third Occupation, you may immediately pay 1 Food to play another Occupation and/or immediately play a Minor Improvement by paying its cost.

Name: Cheese Fondue
Card Text: Whenever you Bake 1 or more Grain into bread, you receive 1 additional Food if you have at least 1 Sheep and 1 additional Food if you have at least 1 Cattle.

Name: Chemist
Card Text: Whenever you use a person’s action to take Wood from a Building resource Accumulation space, you may immediately place 1 Clay from your supply on another Building resource Accumulation space to receive 2 Food.

Name: Cherry Orchard
Card Text: When you Sow, you may plant up to 1 Wood on this card as if it were Grain. Whenever you Harvest the last Wood from this card, you also receive 1 Vegetable. (This card does not count as a field when scoring.)

Name: Chess Player
Card Text: When you use the r Reed action space you also receive 1 Stone. When you use the 1 Sheep action space you also receive 1 Wild Boar.

Name: Chicken Coop
Card Text: Place 1 food each on the next 8 remaining round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

Name: Chief
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each room in your Stone house. (In total, you receive 3 points per room instead of 2.)

Name: Chief’s Daughter
Card Text: If another player plays the 'Chief' card, you can play this card immediately at no cost. At the end of the game, you receive 3 bonus points if you have a Stone house, 1 if you have a Clay hut.
Bonus Points: 3 for stone house, 1 for clay hut

Name: Child Care Worker
Card Text: Whenever another player uses a ”Family Growth” action, you may convert 1 Wood to 2 Food, or 1 Wood and 1 Reed to 5 Food.

Name: Child Psychologist
Card Text: Whenever another player grows their family, you can pay 2 Food to the supply to play an Occupation.

Name: Children’s Playground
Card Text: From now on, one of your pastures can not contain animals.

Name: Child’s Toy
Card Text: During the Feeding phase of each Harvest, your newborns require 2 Food (instead of 1).

Name: Chimney
Card Text: Whenever you build an Oven or a Cooking Hearth, you pay up to 2 building resources less of your choice.

Name: Chimney Sweep
Card Text: Immediately when playing Chimney Sweep and whenever you use the ”Day Laborer” Action space, you receive 1 additional Food for every player with an Oven and 1 additional Food for every player with a Stone house.

Name: Chocolade
Card Text: Place 4 Food from the supply on this card. This food can only be used to pay for Occupations.

Name: Chronicle
Card Text: Place 1 food on each remaining major improvement on the major improvements board. When these improvements are acquired, you receive the food.

Name: Church Warden
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 wood. At the end of the game, any player who performed actions with at least 5 people in Round 14 receives 3 bonus points.

Name: Circus Manager
Card Text: Whenever you use your last person to choose the ”Traveling Players” action on an Action space, you may immediately afterward pay 1 Food to play 1 Occupation.

Name: Clapper
Card Text: Whenever you use one of the family growth action spaces (or if you already have used one of these spaces in this round when you play the Clapper), place 1 additional grain on all of your fields that already contains at least 1 grain.

Name: Clay Deliveryman
Card Text: Place 1 clay on each of the spaces for rounds 6 to 14. At the start of these rounds, take the clay.

Name: Clay Deposit
Card Text: This card is an additional action space. A player who uses this action space must pay you 1 food and receives 5 clay. If you use the Clay Deposit yourself, you may choose to take 2 bonus points instead of the clay.

Name: Clay Digger
Card Text: Include the '1 Clay' Action card from the 3-player game as an additional clay pit. Immediately place 3 clay on the card and add 1 clay at the start of each round. Any player who uses this action must pay you 3 food.

Name: Clay Exchange
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately receive 3 Clay. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Clay Firer
Card Text: You can use the Clay Firer at any time to convert 2 (3) clay to 1 (2) stone.

Name: Clay Hut Builder
Card Text: Once you live in a Clay hut, place 2 clay on each of the next 5 remaining Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, take the clay.

Name: Clay Hut Extension
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately extend your clay hut by 1 room. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Clay Mixer
Card Text: Whenever one of your people's actions gives you only clay, take 2 additional clay.

Name: Clay Path
Card Text: The player with the most valuable street receives 2 bonus points when scoring.

Name: Clay Pit
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Day Labourer” action space, you receive 3 additional clay.

Name: Clay Plasterer
Card Text: Renovating your Wooden hut to a Clay hut costs you only 1 clay and 1 reeds. Each room of your Clay hut costs you 3 clay and 2 reeds.

Name: Clay Roof
Card Text: You can replace 1 or 2 reeds with the same amount of clay whenever you extend or renovate your home.

Name: Clay Seller
Card Text: You can use the Clay Seller at any time to convert 2 clay to 1 sheep or 1 reeds, 3 clay to 1 wild boar or 1 stone and/or 4 clay to 1 cattle.

Name: Clay Starter
Card Text: When you play this card, if you only have 2 rooms in your wooden hut, immediately pay 1 food to renovate it to clay for free.

Name: Clay Supports
Card Text: Whenever you add a room to your clay hut, you can pay 2 clay, 1 wood and 1 reeds instead of 5 clay and 2 reeds.

Name: Claytablet
Card Text: At the start of each Round, before Work phase, you may pay 2 Clay to immediately play 1 Occupation.

Name: Clay Worker
Card Text: Whenever you use an action to take wood or clay, you also receive 1 additional clay.

Name: Clay Worker’s Shop
Card Text: Each Harvest you may convert 1 Wood plus 1 Clay to 4 Food. At the end of the game you may return 2/4/5 pairs of 1 Wood and 1 Clay for 1/2/3 Bonus points.

Name: Clear Cutting
Card Text: Whenever you plough 1 or more fields, you receive 1 wood from the general supply for each field you just ploughed.

Name: Clerk
Card Text: Immediately select a future round space and subtract the number of the current round from it. Place this many Food from the supply on that space. At the start of that round, you receive the Food.

Name: Clockwork Man
Card Text: Clockwork Man counts as 1 Occupation and 1 Minor Improvement.

Name: Clogs
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for a clay hut, or two for a stone house.
Bonus Points: 1 for clay hut, 2 for stone house

Name: Cloister
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each of the 5 columns of your farmyard that contains a room.

Name: Cloister Dweller
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each of the 5 columns of your farmyard that is either all rooms, all fields, or all in the same pasture.

Name: Coal Investor
Card Text: If you play this card in Stage 1, place 4 Wood and 4 Clay on the space for Round 9. At the start of this round, you receive the resources.

Name: Coal Miner
Card Text: Whenever you use a Stone Accumulation space, you also receive 1 Wood and 1 Clay.

Name: Coal Pit
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Day Laborer” Action space, you receive 1 Building resource of your choice.

Name: Coarse Grain
Card Text: You can convert 2 Wood/2 Clay into 3 Food at any time if you chose Wood/Clay as the cost of this card. (Mark this card with 1 Wood/Clay from the supply.)

Name: Cocotte
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Day Laborer” Action space, you can also play a Minor Improvement or pay 1 Food to play a Major Improvement.

Name: Coffee Break
Card Text: Place 1 Food on the next 5 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food. If you play an Occupation before receiving the Food, immediately remove this cards remaining Food from future Round spaces.

Name: Coke Stove
Card Text: Each Harvest, you may convert up to 1 Wood to 3 Food. (The Coke Stove cannot cook animals.)

Name: Collar and Chain
Card Text: You may keep two animals of the same type on Collar and Chain.

Name: Commercial Baker
Card Text: The Stone Oven is now a Minor Improvement for you. Once each round, instead of using an Action space, you may place a Family member on this card and receive 3 Grain.

Name: Compost Farmer
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Plow 1 Field” or ”Plow and/or Sow” Action space, you may pay 1 Wood to build 1 Stable.

Name: Conjurer
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Travelling Players' action on an Action space, take 1 additional grain.

Name: Conservator
Card Text: You can renovate your wooden hut to a stone house without first needing to renovate it to a clay hut.

Name: Consolation Prize
Card Text: You receive 1 building resource of each type in which all the revealed respective Accumulation spaces are occupied. (To receive Stone, at least 1 Stone Accumulation space must be revealed.) AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Constable
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 Rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 wood. At the end of the game, any player who has no negative points receives 5 bonus points.

Name: Contractor
Card Text: Whenever you build a room, renovate your home, or build a Major Improvement, you can replace up to 1 Building resource of your choice with 1 Food.

Name: Convict Number 24601
Card Text: Starting from next round, you cannot take actions with 1 of your Family members. At the end of the game, you receive 2 Bonus Points for every round after playing this card, including the current round.

Name: Cook
Card Text: In the Feeding phase of each Harvest, only 2 of your people eat 2 food each; any others are satisfied with only 1.

Name: Cookies
Card Text: For each Grain that you Bake, you may place 1 Food from your supply in 1 of your rooms. You may have a maximum of 1 Food in each room. You cannot use these Food any more, but each is worth 1 Bonus point at the end of the game.

Name: Cooking Corner
Card Text: At any time, you may convert goods to food as follows: vegetables →4 food; sheep →2 food; wild boar →3 food; cattle →4 food. Whenever you use a bread baking action, you may convert: grain →3 food.

Name: Cooking Hearth
Card Text: At any time, you may convert goods to food as follows: vegetable →3 food; sheep →2 food; wild boar →3 food; cattle →4 food. Whenever you use a bread baking action, you may convert: grain →3 food.

Name: Cooking Instructor
Card Text: Whenever another player builds an Improvement, you may immediately convert 1 Good to Food allowed by that Improvement, which may include Baking bread.

Name: Cook Pot
Card Text: Place 1 Food on the next 3 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.

Name: Copse
Card Text: When you sow, you can plant wood on this card. There can be up to 2 stacks of wood on this card, as shown. The wood is treated the same as sown grain and is harvested during the field phase.

Name: Corn on the Cob
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 4 Food. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Corn Profiteer
Card Text: You can convert 1 grain to 3 food at any time with the Corn Profiteer. Any other player can stop this by paying you 2 food for the grain.

Name: Cornrick
Card Text: Place 1 Grain each on the spaces for rounds 7 and 9. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Grain.

Name: Corn Scoop
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Take 1 grain' Action space, you receive 1 additional grain.

Name: Corn Sheaf
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 1 grain. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Corn Storehouse
Card Text: Whenever you have empty fields after the field phase of the harvest, you can sow grain in them immediately. Place 1 fewer grain from the supply on these fields.

Name: Cornucopia
Card Text: During the Feeding phase of the Harvest, you receive 1 Grain/Vegetable if you chose Grain/Vegetable as the cost of this card. (Mark this card with 1 Grain/Vegetable from the supply.)

Name: Corral
Card Text: You may keep up to 2 animals of the same type on each unsowed field orthogonally adjacent to your home. Different corrals may hold different types of animals. At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each of these fields that holds at least 1 animal.

Name: Costermonger’s Barrow
Card Text: During the Feeding phase of each Harvest, you may convert up to 1 Vegetable to 1 Bonus point and 2 Food.

Name: Cottar
Card Text: Whenever, at the start of a round, no other player has less Food than you, you receive 1 Wood.

Name: Cotton Field
Card Text: You may immediately Plow 1 field.

Name: Country Doctor
Card Text: Once during the game, when you have 3 planted Grain fields, you may play the ”Family growth” action as ”Family growth even without room”.

Name: Countryman
Card Text: After all players have placed their Family members, you may move one of your Family members from a 'Take 1 Grain' or 'Take 1 Vegetable' Action space to a free 'Sowing' Action space after you have taken the Grain or Vegetables.

Name: Country Silo
Card Text: Whenever another player (or, in Solo game, you) uses the ”3 Wood” Action space, you receive 1 Grain.

Name: Covetous Farmer
Card Text: Once each round, during one of your turns, you may take 1 Building resource from the general supply of a type that you do not have and every other player does have.

Name: Cow Bell
Card Text: You may keep up to 2 cattle on each unused space on your farmyard, to a maximum of 4 cattle.

Name: Cowboy
Card Text: You can use the ”Take 1 Sheep”, ”Wild boar” or ”Cattle” Action spaces, even if they are occupied. If you do this, take 1 animal of that type. Additionally, you can keep 2 animals of the same type on 1 of your Unused farmyard spaces. (The space still counts as used.)

Name: Cowboy And Mother
Card Text: From Round 4 on, at the end of any Work phase in which you used 2 or fewer Action spaces and have 2 or fewer Family members, you receive 1 Food. From Round 8/12, you may choose to receive an animal/Plow 1 field instead.

Name: Cow Chips
Card Text: Whenever you Sow a field that is orthogonally adjacent to a pasture, you may place extra good from the supply on it.

Name: Cowherd
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Cattle' Action space, take 1 additional cattle from the supply.

Name: Crib
Card Text: At the end of any Work phase in which you have taken a ”Family Growth” action, you may take 2 Food from the general supply or pay 2 Food for 1 Bonus point.

Name: Crop Rotation Field
Card Text: When you Sow, you may Sow on this card as if it were 1 field. Whenever you remove the last Grain/Vegetable from this card, you may immediately Sow 1 Vegetable/Grain on this card from your supply.

Name: Crop Rotator
Card Text: Whenever you remove the last Grain/Vegetable from a field, you may immediately sow a Vegetable/Grain in that field. When you play this card, immediately take 1 Grain or Vegetable from each of your fields and place them in your supply.

Name: Crystal Ball
Card Text: Whenever any player plays an Occupation or Improvement which allows placement of one or more Building resources, Food, Grain and/or Vegetables on future round spaces, you receive 1 Food.

Name: Custom Improvements
Card Text: You receive 1 Bonus point any time you play a Minor Improvement with an action that could have allowed you to play a Major Improvement instead (including this one).

Name: Dairy Crier
Card Text: When you play this card, each player can choose to receive 2 Sheep or 2 food. You receive 1 additional Cattle.

Name: Dairy Supply
Card Text: When you play this card, you immediately receive 1 Sheep if the ”Fishing” action space is occupied and 1 Cattle if the ”Take 1 Sheep” action space is occupied. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Dancer
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Travelling Players' action on an Action space, you receive at least 4 Food, even if only 1 to 3 Food are on the space.

Name: Debris Dealer
Card Text: When you renovate your Clay hut, you receive 2 Clay afterward. Once you live in a Clay hut or Stone house, whenever another player renovates their Wooden/Clay hut, you receive 2 Wood/Clay.

Name: Debt Collector
Card Text: When you play this card, place a Guest token on any Round space at least 3 higher than the current round. At the start of that round, pay the Starting player (which may be you) 1 Food and place the Guest. After that, play starts with the starting player as usual.

Name: Dentist
Card Text: When you play this card and at the end of each Harvest, you may place 1 Wood on this card from your supply. At the start of each Harvest, you receive 1 Food for every Wood on this card.

Name: Diamond Mine
Card Text: Place 1 Stone each on the next 2 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Stone.

Name: Diary
Card Text: Whenever you play an Occupation, you receive 1 Wood.

Name: Dictionary
Card Text: You may use the ”Occupation” Action on an Action space even if it is occupied. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point if you have 7 played Occupations.

Name: Diligent Farmer
Card Text: When you play this card, if you would score the maximum 4 points in 3 scoring categories (including fenced stables), you can extend your home by 1 room at no cost.

Name: Dinghy
Card Text: Immediately take all the food from the ”Fishing” accumulation space and place an equal amount of wood from the general supply on the space afterward. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Diplomat
Card Text: Once during round 14, you may use an Action space even if it is occupied by other players. If you play this card in Round 7 or before, you may do this twice instead.

Name: Dock Worker
Card Text: At any time, you can use the Dock Worker to convert 3 wood to either 1 clay, 1 reeds or 1 stone, or to convert 2 clay, 2 reeds or 2 stone to one other resource.

Name: Dolly’s Mother
Card Text: You only require 1 Sheep to breed during the Breeding phase of a Harvest. This card can hold Sheep.

Name: Donkey
Card Text: From now on, one of your pastures can not contain animals. (Mark this pasture with 1 Clay from the general supply.) Whenever you use the ”Plow 1 field” or ”Plow 1 field and/or Sow” Action space, you may Plow 1 additional field.

Name: Dormer
Card Text: Pay 1 Reed less to build each room, for each Renovation, and for each Improvement.

Name: Dough
Card Text: When you use a ”Bake bread” action, you may discard this card for 5 Food.

Name: Dovecote
Card Text: Place 1 Food each on the spaces for rounds 10 to 14. At the start of these rounds, take the food.

Name: Dove Hunter
Card Text: In the Feeding phase of each Harvest, the Chandler can convert either 1 Sheep to 4 Food or 1 Cattle to 6 Food.

Name: Dovetail Joints
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 2 Food. From now on, the Joinery is a Minor Improvement for you and costs you nothing.

Name: Downsizing
Card Text: Once during the game, when you build at least 1 fence, you may return 1 Wooden room that you built this game and build up to 7 additional fences from your supply at no cost. If there was a person in that room, move them into the rest of your home.

Name: Dragonfly Pond
Card Text: Place 1 Food on each of the next 2 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.

Name: Drained Pond
Card Text: Add 4 to the current round and place 1 field on the corresponding Round space. At the start of that round, you may pay 1 Food to add the field to your farm.

Name: Drawing Genius
Card Text: Immediately after using the most recent Round card, you may play 1 Major or Minor Improvement. (Pay costs for the Improvement normally.)

Name: Dray
Card Text: Whenever you use a Person to take exactly 1 resource from a Building resource Accumulation space, you receive an additional 1 Building resource of your choice and 1 Food.

Name: Driftwood
Card Text: Place 1 Wood each on the next 2 Round spaces. At the start of the round, you receive the Wood.

Name: Drinker of Absinthe
Card Text: Place 1 Food each on the spaces for rounds 7 to 14. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.

Name: Drinking Trough
Card Text: Each Pasture (with or without a stable) can hold up to 2 more animals.

Name: Drunkard
Card Text: At the start of each Round, you may pay 1 Food. If you do, at the end of each Work phase, you may move 1 Person from the ”Take 1 Grain” Action space to an unoccupied Action space and take that action.

Name: Dual Field
Card Text: On this card, you can plant 1 Grain and/or 1 Vegetables as you would on a field. (This card does not count as a field when scoring.)

Name: Duck Pond
Card Text: Place 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

Name: Dung Collector
Card Text: Whenever you breed 2 or more newborn animals, you may pay 1 Food to Plow 1 field.

Name: Dunghill
Card Text: Whenever you have 2/3 different types of animals at the beginning of the field phase, you may add 1 more resource on 1/2 of your planted fields (or planted cards).

Name: Dusk Worker
Card Text: You may immediately Plow 1 field and build 1 or 2 free fences.

Name: Eager Student
Card Text: Whenever you use the most recent Round card with your last person, you may immediately afterward pay 1 Food to play 1 Occupation.

Name: Early Riser
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 1 sheep. For you, the breeding phase of the harvest precedes the field and feeding phases. You may immediately cook baby animals if you do not have room to keep them.

Name: Edible Roots
Card Text: Whenever you harvest the last good from a field and place it in your supply, you receive 1 Food.

Name: Educated Sheep
Card Text: Choose two - receive 1 Grain; or receive 2 Food; or build 1 stable for 1 Wood; or receive 1 Building Resource of your choice. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Educator
Card Text: Whenever another player plays an Occupation card, you can pay 3 food to play one yourself. From your 4th Occupation, this only costs 2 food.

Name: Eel Farm
Card Text: Whenever you take the ”Fishing” action space, you may place 1 Clay from the general supply on an unused farmyard space. This space is now considered used, can hold up to 1 Animal, and forms a natural border for pastures.

Name: Elder’s Daughter
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 Clay. At the end of the game, all players with the most Major Improvements receive 3 bonus points each.

Name: Employee of the Month
Card Text: Place different Goods on each of the next Round spaces, up to the number of your played Occupations. (Nor counting this one.) At the start of these rounds, you receive the Good.

Name: Encircling Wall
Card Text: When you play this card, you may immediately fence 1 space in your farmyard. (You do not need to pay Wood for the fences).

Name: Engineer
Card Text: Whenever you play an Improvement, you receive 1 Food before paying the costs of the Improvement. If the Improvement has a printed Stone cost, you receive 2 Food instead of 1.

Name: Engineering
Card Text: When you play this card, you may immediately take a ”Renovation” action. Pay 1 Building resource less for each completed Round of the game.

Name: Entrepreneur
Card Text: At the start of each round, you may move 1 Food from your supply to this card or 1 Food from this card to the general supply. If you do either, you receive 1 building resource of a type you do not currently have.

Name: Eremite
Card Text: At the start of each round, if you have no played Minor Improvements in front of you, you receive 1 Food. At the start of each Harvest, if you have no played Minor Improvements in front of you, you receive 1 Wood.

Name: Errant Oven
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately take the ”Bake bread” a bread baking action.

Name: Estate Manager
Card Text: At the end of a 3/4/5 player game, if no player has more animals of any type than you, you receive 2/3/4 bonus points.

Name: Evening Prayer
Card Text: If you have 1/2 plowed fields, immediately pay 1/2 Food to play 1/2 Occupations.

Name: Executive Boar
Card Text: Choose two - receive 1 Vegetable; or receive 3 Food; or build up to 6 fences for 4 Wood; or receive a ‘Family Growth with Room‘ action. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Expansion Plans
Card Text: Add 4 to the round number and place 1 Stone Room on that Action space. At the start of that Round, you may expand your Stone House by 1 Room for free.

Name: Experienced Farmer
Card Text: Pile (from bottom to top) 1 Cattle, Cattle, Boar, Sheep, Vegetable, Grain on this card. At the start of each Harvest in which you have more plowed fields than any of the other players, you receive the top item.

Name: Expert Builder
Card Text: Whenever any player (including you) builds 1 or more rooms, they must place 1 resource of those paid on this card. You may use these resources to pay for your extensions or renovations.

Name: Extensive Resume
Card Text: At the end of the game you receive 1/2/3 Bonus points for 5/6/7 played Occupations.

Name: Extra Effort
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately build a major improvement.

Name: Family Counselor
Card Text: Whenever at the end of a work phase all of your adult family members were placed on the same game board, you receive 1 food/1 grain/1 vegetable if it were 2/3/4+ adults.

Name: Family Physician
Card Text: Whenever you use an action space orthogonally adjacent to 2/3/4+ action spaces occupied by other players Family members, you receive 1 Food/1 Grain/1 Vegetable before you take the action.

Name: Family Planner
Card Text: Add 4, 7 and 10 to the current round and place up to 1 person still in your supply on each corresponding Round space. At the start of these rounds and not earlier, you may take the person as a new Family member, if you have room. (If you don’t, return it to your supply.) It counts as a Newborn, and it may take actions the following round.

Name: Farmer
Card Text: The next time you build fences, take 1 wild boar. Each time you build at least 1 fence after that, take 1 cattle marker.

Name: Farmers Market
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 1 vegetable.

Name: Farmer’s Straw Hat
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately take the Field phase of a Harvest on your farmyard.

Name: Farm Name Sign
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 2 Bonus points, if your farm has no unused spaces.

Name: Farm Overseer
Card Text: Whenever you Plow at least 1 field you receive 1 Food for each stable on your farm. Whenever you build at least 1 stable you receive 1 Food for each plowed field on your farm.

Name: Farm School
Card Text: Whenever you harvest at least 2 goods during the Field phase of a Harvest, you may immediately afterward pay 2 Food to play an Occupation.

Name: Farm School Graduate
Card Text: You may only play this card as your first Occupation. From now on, you may not use any Occupation action on any Action space. At the start of each Harvest, you may pay 2 Food to play an Occupation.

Name: Farm Stable
Card Text: Place 1 Food each on the next 2/3/4/5 remaining Round spaces if you have 1/2/3/4 stables. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.

Name: Farm Steward
Card Text: Once you live in a Clay hut or Stone house, play your next Family growth action as 'Family growth even without room in the hut or house'.

Name: Farm Worker
Card Text: Whenever you Plow 1 or more fields, you may convert 1 building resource into 3 Food for each field you just plowed.

Name: Feed Pellets
Card Text: During the feeding phase of each harvest, you may trade 1 vegetable for 1 of any type of animal that you already have in your farmyard.

Name: Fence Builder
Card Text: When you play this card, place one of your fences on an Action space of your choice. If you use an action on this Action space, you can also build fences as an additional action.

Name: Fence Dealer
Card Text: You may only build 12 fences. (Remove the other 3 fences from the game.) Whenever you build fences, you may build 2 fences for every 1 Wood paid.

Name: Fence Deliveryman
Card Text: Add 6 and 10 to the current Round. Place 4 of your fences on each corresponding Round space. At the start of these rounds, you can pay 2 food to build all 4 fences immediately.

Name: Fence Helper
Card Text: Whenever another player takes a ”Build fences” fence building action, you may pay that player 1 food to immediately take the ”Fences” a fence building action too.

Name: Fence Overseer
Card Text: Each Round, for a cost of 1 food, you can immediately fence a pasture of 1 farmyard space around a stable that you have just built. You do not need to pay wood for the fences.

Name: Fencer
Card Text: Whenever another player builds 1 to 4 fences, take 1 wood from the supply. Whenever another player builds 5 or more fences, take 2 wood.

Name: Fencing Master
Card Text: Whenever you play an Occupation (including this one), place up to 2 fences from your supply on this card. From your 4th Occupation on, place up to 1 fence instead. You build these fences for free when taking a ”Fences” Action.

Name: Festival Hall
Card Text: After the Field phase of a Harvest, you can use the Festival Hall to convert 1 Grain to 1 Building resource of your choice and 2 Food.

Name: Fiddler
Card Text: At the end of each round in which you did not receive any Building resources, you receive 1 Wood. If its an evennumbered round, you additionally receive 1 Food.

Name: Field
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately plough 1 field. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left.

Name: Field Agent
Card Text: When you Plow at least 1 field, you can pay 2 food to get 1 Grain/Vegerable or do a Sow action.

Name: Field Barterer
Card Text: When you play this card, you may place up to 4 different building resources on it from your supply. At the start of each harvest, you may return a resource from this card to the supply to Plow 1 field.

Name: Field Doctor
Card Text: Once during the game, if you live in a 2 room home surrounded by 4 plowed fields, you may play the ”Family growth” action as ”Family growth even without room”.

Name: Fieldsman
Card Text: Whenever you sow 1 field, place 2 extra goods from the supply on the newly sown field. Whenever you sow 2 fields, place 1 extra good on each.

Name: Field Warden
Card Text: You can use the 'Take 1 vegetable', 'Plough fields' and 'Plough fields and Sow' actions even if another player has placed a person on the space.

Name: Field Watchman
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Take 1 grain' Action space, you can also plough up to 1 field.

Name: Field Worker
Card Text: Whenever another player sows one or more fields, you receive 1 grain in a 3-player game, 1 food in a 4 or 5 player game.

Name: Fisherman
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Catch Fish' Action space, you can take twice as many food as are on the space. You then give 1 food each to the owners of the Fishing Rod, Raft, Canoe, Fish Trap and Landing Net.

Name: Fisherman’s Apprentice
Card Text: Whenever another player uses the ”Fishing” action, you may pay that player 1 food to play an occupation.

Name: Fish Hook
Card Text: Whenever you use an Action space orthogonally adjacent to the ”Fishing” Action space, you may immediately take 1 Food from the ”Fishing” Action space if there is a Food on that space.

Name: Fishing Rod
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Catch Fish' Action space, you receive 1 additional food. From Round 8, you receive 2 additional food.

Name: Fish Pond
Card Text: Place 1 Clay from the general supply on 1 Unused farmyard space. It now counts as used. Whenever you use the ”Fishing” Action space, you may place some or all of the Food on this card. At the end of the game, you receive -1/1/2/3/4 Bonus points for 0/1/4/6/8 Food on this card.

Name: Fish Seller
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Fishing” action, you may use food that you take from that action space to buy up to 2 items. One building resource costs 1 food. One grain, sheep or wild boar costs 2 food. One vegetable or cattle costs 3 food.

Name: Fish Trap
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Fishing” action space or receive reed on an action space, you receive 1 additional food.

Name: Five Rings
Card Text: Whenever you play an Improvement, pay 1 Wood/Stone less if you chose Wood/Stone as the cost of this card. (Mark this card with 1 Wood/ Stone from the supply.)

Name: Flagon
Card Text: Whenever the Well A10 is built or upgraded to a Village Well I66, you receive 4 food, and the other players receive 1 food each. If the Well has already been built, everyone receives the food when the Flagon is played.

Name: Flail
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Plough 1 Field” or ”Plough Field and Sow” action spaces, you can additionally bake bread.

Name: Flamingo Lake
Card Text: Place 4 Food on this a from the general supply. At the start of each Work Phase, if you are the last player in turn order, move 1 Food from this card to your supply.

Name: Flat Hill
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately Renovate your Wooden hut to a Clay hut for free.

Name: Flea Market
Card Text: Place 1 Wood/Clay/Reed from your supply on an unoccupied Action space of your choice to receive 2/2/3 Food. The next player to use this Action space receives the resource. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Fleece Factory
Card Text: Whenever you breed a Sheep, you receive 1 Bonus point.

Name: Flower Pots
Card Text: If you play this in Round 6 or before, place 1 Vegetable on the space for Round 8. At the start of this round, you receive the Vegetable.

Name: Food Bank
Card Text: Place 1 of your unbuilt stables on an Action space of your choice. You cannot build that stable anymore. Whenever another player uses that Action space, you receive 1 Food from the general supply.

Name: Food Connoisseur
Card Text: Pile (from bottom to top) 1 Clay, 1 Vegetable, 1 Stone, 1 Grain, 1 Wood and 1 Reed on this card. Whenever you use a person to take Food from the ”Day Laborer”, ”Fishing” or ”Traveling Players” Action space, additionally take the top item on this card.

Name: Food Critic
Card Text: The next time you use the ”Fishing” action, place 1 food from the general supply on this card. Whenever you bake bread or convert animals, vegetables, or building resources to food, you may place that good on this card. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/4/6 bonus points for 4/5/6/7+ different items on this card.

Name: Food Storehouse
Card Text: Place 2 Food on each of the spaces for Rounds 8, 10, 12 and 14. At the start of these Rounds, you receive the Food.

Name: Football Player
Card Text: Place a marker on the Round 1 Action space. Whenever you use the marked space, pay 0/1/2 Food and move the marker to the Round space that is 1/2/3 rounds greater. At the end of the game, you receive Bonus points equal to the Stage number where the marker is.

Name: Foraging
Card Text: Once per round you may return any number of sown Grain and/or Vegetables to the general supply. For each good returned, you receive 1 Food.

Name: Foreign Labor
Card Text: Place 1 Guest token on each of the next 3 even Round spaces. At the start of these Rounds, you receive the Guest (which you may place as you would a person).

Name: Foreman
Card Text: At the start of the Work phase, you can place 1 Food from the general supply on an Action space of your choice.

Name: Foresight
Card Text: The next Improvement you play costs 2 Building resources less.

Name: Forester
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Sowing' action, you can plant up to a maximum of 3 wood on this card. The wood is treated the same as sown grain and is harvested during the Field phase.

Name: Forest Farmer
Card Text: Place 3 Arrow markers on this card. Each round, between the Work and Returning home phase, you may pay 1 Wood and return 1 marker to move one of your people from the ”Plow 1 field” or ”Plow 1 field and/or Sow” Action space to a free Action space and take the action.

Name: Forest Pasture
Card Text: This card can hold an unlimited number of wild boar.

Name: Frame Builder
Card Text: In each renovation, you may replace exactly 1 clay or 1 stone with 1 wood. In each extension, you may replace exactly 2 clay or 2 stone with 1 wood.

Name: Freeloader
Card Text: Each round, your first family member may use an occupied action space. Your first family member can never take the ”Starting Player” action space.

Name: Free-Range Chickens
Card Text: At the start of each round, if you have at least 1 Grain field, place 1 Food from the general supply on this card. You may use this Food at any time. At the end of the game you receive 1/2 Bonus Points for 3/6 Food on this card.

Name: Fresh Dirt Worker
Card Text: At the start of each round if you have exactly 1/2/3 empty plowed field(s) you receive 1 Clay/Wood/building resource of your choice.

Name: Fresh Fence Worker
Card Text: At the start of each round, if you have exactly 1/3/4/5 empty fenced pasture(s), you receive 1 Food/Sheep/Grain/Animal of your choice.

Name: Friendly Pastures
Card Text: Your pastures with stables may hold 2 different animal types. (This card does not change the capacity of your pastures.)

Name: Friendly Worker
Card Text: Each round, if you have a person on the ”Day Laborer” Action space you may use non-accumulating Round 1-9 Action spaces even if they are occupied.

Name: Fruitcake
Card Text: In the Feeding phase of the next Harvest, each of your family members (including newborns) require 1 Food less.

Name: Fruit Tart
Card Text: When you take the ”Take 1 Grain” or ”Take 1 Vegetable” action, you may also take the ”Bake bread” action.

Name: Fruit Tree
Card Text: Place 1 food on each remaining Round space for rounds 8 to 14. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

Name: Fruit Tree House
Card Text: Whenever you extend your home, you may place 1 Food from the supply on this card for each new room. During the Feeding phase of the Harvest, you receive 1 Food from the supply for each Food on this card.

Name: Full-bottomed Wig
Card Text: You no longer need Reed when you renovate your home.

Name: Gardener
Card Text: Take vegetables from the supply and not from your vegetable field whenever you harvest them - you keep the vegetables on the fields.

Name: Gardener’s Wife
Card Text: Whenever you grow your Family, you receive 1 Vegetable from the supply.

Name: Gardening Enthusiast
Card Text: You immediately receive a loan of 1 Grain and 1 Vegetable. Before scoring, return the 2 crops from your supply. For each crop that you cannot or choose not to return, you receive 1 Negative point.

Name: Gardening Tools
Card Text: Whenever another player sows, you may pay 1 food to the supply to immediately take a ”Sow” action.

Name: Garden Statue
Card Text: Pile (from bottom to top) 1 Vegetable, Stone, Grain, Stone, Grain on this card. You receive the top item when you use the ”Day Laborer” Action space.

Name: Gelder
Card Text: Place 4 Food on this card. Whenever you use an Animal accumulation space with exactly 1 Animal on it, you may remove 1 of these Food, to receive 1 Stone and 1 Bonus point.

Name: Gem Hunter
Card Text: Whenever you use a person to take Clay from a Building resource Accumulation space, you may also receive 1 Stone. In Round 9 or before, if there are 3 or more Clay on the space, you must leave 1 Clay on the space to receive the Stone.

Name: Giant
Card Text: Each round you may take one of your people’s actions twice. If you use an Accumulation space with that action, you get double the amount of Goods and/or Food. Each round, starting next round, you must place 1 fewer person.

Name: Giant Scarecrow
Card Text: Take Grain from the supply and not your Grain field whenever you harvest them - you keep the Grain on the fields.

Name: Gimlet
Card Text: Pay 1 less wood/clay/stone for each wood/clay/stone room. Each round you build stables, pay 1 less wood for up to 2 stables.

Name: Glass Blower
Card Text: If you have an oven, the Glassblower may convert 1/2 clay to 3/5 food in each harvest.

Name: Glassblowers Shop
Card Text: Each harvest you may convert up to 1 Clay into 3 Food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 Bonus points for 2/4/5 Clay. (A repurchased Pottery does not give you additional Bonus points, but can be used to convert an additional Clay each Harvest.)

Name: Gleaner
Card Text: You receive 1 building resource of your choice whenever you harvest the last good on a Field.

Name: Gluten Free Baker
Card Text: You may not convert Grain to Food for the rest of the game. For you, the ”Bake bread” action reads ”Convert up to 2 Vegetables to 4 Food and 1 Bonus point each”. When you play this card, you may take a ”Bake bread” Action.

Name: Glutton
Card Text: In the feeding phase of the last harvest after feeding your family normally, you may pay 3 additional food for 1 bonus point, up to a maximum of 6 bonus points.

Name: Goblet
Card Text: The Well is a Minor Improvement for you. When you build the Well, place 2 Food on the next 5 Round spaces instead of 1 Food. (If you have already built the Well, then this card has no effect.)

Name: Godfather
Card Text: Whenever you use an Action space orthogonally adjacent to an occupied ”Family Growth” Action space from stage 2 or stage 5, you receive 1 Grain immediately before the action is resolved.

Name: Golden Rose
Card Text: Whenever you pay Food to play an Occupation or a Minor Improvement, you may pay up to 2 Food less.

Name: Gold Medal
Card Text: When you play this card, choose one category from Grain, Vegetables, Sheep, Wild boar, Cattle, fields, pastures, or fences stables. At the end of the game, all players with the most Points in that category receive 2 Bonus points.

Name: Good Friend
Card Text: Place 3 Wood, 2 Clay, 2 Stone, and 1 Reed on this card. Whenever another player pays building resources to build a Minor Improvement, you may take 1 building resource of the same type from this card.

Name: Goose Pond
Card Text: Place 1 food each on the next 4 remaining round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

Name: Graduate
Card Text: If this is your 7th occupation, this card provides room for one Family member.

Name: Grafter
Card Text: During the breeding phase of harvest, if you have 2 or more grain in your personal supply, you receive 1 grain from the general supply. If you have 2 or more vegetables, you receive 1 vegetable from the general supply.

Name: Grain Bag
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Take 1 Grain” Action space, you receive 1 additional Grain for each baking Improvement you have.

Name: Grain Cart
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Take 1 Grain” action space, you receive 2 additional grain.

Name: Grain Elevator
Card Text: At the start of each round, add 1 Grain from the general supply to this card, unless it has Grain equal to the number of players. Whenever you use the ”Take 1 Grain” action, you also take all the Grain on this card. Whenever another player uses the ”Take 1 Grain” action, return all Grain on this card to the general supply.

Name: Grain Farmer
Card Text: Whenever you have at least 1/4/7/10 planted grain at the beginning of the field phase of a harvest, you receive 1/2/3/4 food.

Name: Grain Lover
Card Text: If there are still 7 full rounds to play, you may plow 1 field, which you may immediately sow with 1 grain from your personal supply. At the end of the game, all players with the most total grain receive 2 bonus points each.

Name: Grain Market
Card Text: Whenever you harvest at least 1 grain, you may immediately afterward exchange 1 grain from your personal supply for 1 vegetable.

Name: Grain Mill Oven
Card Text: You can’t Bake bread with the Grain Mill Oven. Clay and Stone Ovens are Minor Improvements for you. You may discard this instead of paying an Oven’s cost. When you play this card, you can immediately take the ”Bake bread” action.

Name: Grain Seller
Card Text: At any time, you may pay 1/2/3 Grain for 1 Sheep/Boar/Cattle. When you play this card, you may move all your planted Grain to your supply. (This does not count as a harvest.)

Name: Grain Shrine
Card Text: At the end of rounds 8, and 10, you receive 1 Sheep for each Grain field that you have, and you may convert up to 3 Grain in your supply to Wood at a 1:2 ratio.

Name: Grain Speculator
Card Text: Add 1, 3, and 5 to the current round and place 1 Grain on each corresponding Round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Grain.

Name: Granary
Card Text: Place 1 grain on each remaining space for rounds 8, 10 and 12. At the start of these rounds, you receive the grain.

Name: Grantor
Card Text: When you play this card you receive 2 Food for each of your other played Occupations up to a maximum of 8 Food.

Name: Greengrocer
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Take 1 Grain' Action space, also take 1 vegetable.

Name: Greenhouse
Card Text: Add 4 and 7 tothe number of the current round and place 1 vegetable on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you can pay 1 food to take the vegetable.

Name: Grocer
Card Text: Pile (from bottom to top) 1 vegetable, reeds, clay, wood, vegetable, stone, grain, reeds on this card. At any time, you may buy the top item for 1 food.

Name: Groom
Card Text: Once you have a Stone house, you can build 1 stable at a cost of 1 wood at the beginning of each round, without placing a person.

Name: Grotto
Card Text: Place 3 Clay and 2 Stone from the supply on this card. At the end of each round, you may pay 2 Food to receive 1 of these resources. Once this card is empty, it provides room for 1 Family member.

Name: Ground Pickaxe Plow
