Card Text: Once during the game, when you use either the ”Plow 1 field” or the ”Plow 1 field and/or Sow” Action space, you can place 1 Wood from your supply on 1/2 orthogonally adjacent (revealed or unrevealed) Action spaces (to the used plow space) to Plow 1/2 additional fields.

Name: Groupie
Card Text: Whenever another player uses the ”Traveling Players” action on an Action space, you may immediately afterward place 2 Food on that Action space to receive a Guest token to place once that round as you would a person.

Name: Grump
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 wood. At the end of the game, all players with the fewest family members receive 2 binus points each.

Name: Guest
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive a guest token, which you may place once in the next round as you would a person. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Guest Plow
Card Text: Whenever you use either the ”Plow 1 field” or ”Plow 1 field and/or Sow” Action space, you may Plow 1 additional Field for each of your empty Rooms.

Name: Guest Room
Card Text: Immediately place as much Food as you choose from your personal supply on this card. At the start of each round, you may discard 1 Food from this card for a Guest token which you place that round as you would a Family member.

Name: Guild Hall
Card Text: Whenever you build a Major Improvement, you may play 1 Occupation for free.

Name: Guildmaster
Card Text: You receive 4 wood when you acquire the JoineryA7 or play the Cabinetmaker I258. When you acquire the Pottery A8 or play the Potter E214, you receive 4 clay. When you acquire the Basketmaker’s Workshop A9 or play the Basketmaker E183, you receive 3 reed. If you have already played any of these cards when you play the Guildmaster, you receive 2 building resources of the appropriate type for each existing card.

Name: Gym Trainer
Card Text: Whenever you use your 4th or 5th person to take building resource(s) from an Action space, you also receive 1 Wood and 1 Food.

Name: Haberdasher
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 1 Grain. Instead of the ”Bake Bread” action on the corresponding Action space, you may take the ”1 Minor Improvement” action and vice versa. (This card does not affect additional actions on those spaces.)

Name: Half-timbered House
Card Text: At the end of the game you receive 1 bonus point for each room in your Stone house. (In total, you receive 3 points instead of 2 per room.)

Name: Ham Glazer
Card Text: For you, the ”Bake bread” action reads ”Bake Bread and/or convert 1 Wild Boar to 6 Food”. When you play this card, and each time another player takes a ”Bake bread” action, you may convert 1 Wild Boar to 6 Food.

Name: Hammer
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately take a ”Fences” action. Pay 1 wood to every other player who has at least 1 fence.

Name: Hammock
Card Text: One of your rooms can hold 1 additional Family member.

Name: Handmaid
Card Text: Once during the game, when you have all the types of animals, you may play the ”Family growth” action as ”Family growth even without room”

Name: Hand Mill
Card Text: During the feeding phase of the harvest, you can use the Hand Mill to turn either 1 grain into 2 food or 2 grain into 4 food.

Name: Harbor Master
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Fishing” action space, you receive 1 additional reed. Whenever another player uses the ”Fishing” action space, you receive 1 reed and 1 food from the general supply.

Name: Harrow
Card Text: Once during the game, when you use either the ”Plough 1 Field” or the ”Plough Field and Sow” action space, you can plough 2 fields instead of 1. Each other player can also do this once during the game, but must pay you 2 food to do it.

Name: Harvester
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 Wood. At the end of the game, all players who harvest at least 5 goods during the final Harvest receive 3 Bonus points.

Name: Harvest Feast
Card Text: When you play this card, each player may immediately move any amount of their planted resources to their personal supply.

Name: Harvest Helper
Card Text: At the start of the Feeding phase in each Harvest, you can take 1 grain from 1 field belonging to another player. S/he takes 2 food from the supply.

Name: Harvest Totem
Card Text: Whenever you play an Occupation, you receive 1 Wild Boar afterward.

Name: Hasty Trader
Card Text: When you play this card, you may convert as many building resources to Food as you have Family members. For each Wood or Clay, you receive 2 Food, for each Stone or Reed, you receive 3 Food.

Name: Hayloft
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1/2 bonus points for 3/4 stables, 1 bonus point if you have 3 stables, or 2 bonus points if you have 4 stables.

Name: Hay Rake
Card Text: Each Work phase, the third person you place can use the ”Plow 1 field” Action space even if it is occupied.

Name: Haystack
Card Text: Place 3 Food on each remaining Round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.

Name: Headmaster
Card Text: Whenever another player plays an occupation, place 1 food from the supply on this card. Whenever you play an occupation, you may pay any costs using food from this card. At the end of a 4/5 player game, you receive 1 bonus point for every 3/4 food on this card.

Name: Head of the Family
Card Text: You can use any Building or Family growth Action space, even if another player has already placed a person on it.

Name: Head Of The Revolution
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 Wood. After playing this card, the next player to have no Unused farmyard spaces (who currently has Unused spaces) receives 3 Bonus points.

Name: Heatwave
Card Text: Immediately build a Fireplace for free or upgrade a Fireplace to a Cooking Hearth.

Name: Heavy Cart
Card Text: Whenever you take Building resources from an Action space, you receive 1 additional resource of 1 of the taken types.

Name: Heavy Plow
Card Text: Whenever you use either the ”Plow 1 field” or ”Plow 1 field and/or Sow” Action space, you may Plow 1 additional field for every 2 Cattle you have.

Name: Hedge Keeper
Card Text: Whenever you build at least 1 fence, you can build 3 additional fences without paying any additional wood.

Name: Hedge Master
Card Text: Whenever you build fences, you receive 1 Bonus point. When you play this card, you immediately get a ”Build fences” action.

Name: Heiress
Card Text: At the start of round 6/10/13, you may (before phase 1) build 1 Wooden/Clay/Stone room if your home is made of the same material. Pay 1 less Clay for the Clay room, and 2 less Stone for the Stone room. (When you play this card, place the Room tiles on the corresponding round spaces.)

Name: Helper
Card Text: You can use any ”Renovate”, ”Build room(s)” or ”Major Improvement” action space, even if another player has already placed a person on it.

Name: Hen Keeper
Card Text: Whenever you harvest Grain you may place them on this card. The Grain cannot be used anymore but are counted as Grain at the end of the game. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 Bonus points for 2/4/6 Grain on this card.

Name: Herb Garden
Card Text: Place 1 food on each of the next 5 round spaces. At the start of each round, you receive the food.

Name: Herdsman
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Day Laborer” Action space, you receive 1 additional Sheep. From round 6, you can choose to receive 1 Wild boar instead.

Name: Hermit
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 6 bonus points if this is the only occupation you have played.

Name: Hermit’s Stick
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/4 Bonus points for having exactly 4/3/2 Family members.

Name: Hewer
Card Text: From Round 3, at the end of each round in which no player used a person to take Clay from an Action space, you receive 1 Stone and 1 Food.

Name: Hide Farmer
Card Text: At the end of the game, you can pay 1 food each for any number of unused fields. These do not lose you points in the scoring.

Name: Hill Bandit
Card Text: Whenever another player builds an Improvement with a printed Clay cost, you receive 1 Food and 1 Clay.

Name: Hill Cultivator
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Take 1 Grain”/”Take 1 Vegetable” Action space, you receive an additional 2/3 Clay.

Name: Hill Farmer
Card Text: Whenever you build 1 or more fences or Plow 1 or more fields, you receive 2/1 Food if you have exactly 2/3 rooms.

Name: Hired Helpers
Card Text: Each round, you may place 1 guest token as you would a family member. If you do this, you must place 1 fewer person next round. You can not use this ability in consecutive rounds nor in round 14.

Name: Historian
Card Text: ay When you play this card, you may place up to 4 different resources on it from your supply. At the start of each round, you may return a resource from this card to the supply to play an occupation.

Name: Hoarder
Card Text: Each Harvest, you can place 1 Building resource on this card from your supply. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3/5 Bonus points for 1/2/3/4 different resources on this card.

Name: Hobby Farmer
Card Text: Take 1 vegetable when you play this card.

Name: Hobbyist
Card Text: Whenever another player plays an occupation, you may pay 1 food to the general supply to play 1 minor improvement, or 2 food to build 1 major improvement.

Name: Hoe
Card Text: At the end of each Work phase, if you occupy both the ”Take 1 Grain” and ”Take 1 Vegetable” Action spaces, you may immediately Plow 1 field.

Name: Hoe Maker
Card Text: Whenever you build at least 1 fence, you may also pay 1 food to plough 1 field.

Name: Holiday House
Card Text: In round 14, you cannot place any people. Play this card at the latest during round 13.

Name: Holiday Present
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately either discard 1 begging card or take 1 animal (of your choice) from the supply.

Name: Home Garden
Card Text: Whenever you renovate your home, or if you renovated this round, place 1 Food on the next 3 remaining Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.

Name: Homesteader
Card Text: At the start of Rounds 12, 13, and 14, you may Plow 1 field if you live ina Wooden Hut. Unassigned

Name: Homework
Card Text: When you play this card, you may immediately play up to 2 Occupations. (You must still pay any additional costs of the Occupations.)

Name: Honeymoon
Card Text: In Round 14, you place 2 fewer Family members.

Name: Honour Student
Card Text: Pile (from bottom to top) 1 Stone, Vegetable, Wood, Grain, Sheep and Clay on this card. You receive the top item after you play an Occupation.

Name: Horse
Card Text: You receive 2 bonus points for any one type of animal missing from your farm at the end of the game.

Name: Horse Barn
Card Text: Whenever you sow, each field you sow that is orthogonally adjacent to exactly 1 stable gets an additional 1 grain or 1 vegetable. Each field that is orthogonally adjacent to 2 or more stables, gets an additional 2 grain or 2 vegetables.

Name: Horseman
Card Text: At the start of each Harvest, you receive 1 Sheep/Wild boar/Cattle if you have 1/2/3+ fenced stable(s).

Name: House Goat
Card Text: In each feeding phase, you receive 1 food. Apart from the goat, you cannot hold any other animal in your home.

Name: House Steward
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 wood. At the end of the game, players with the most rooms in their home each receive 3 bonus points.

Name: Humble Farmboy
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 Wood. At the end of the game, all players with the fewest cards in front of them receive 3 Bonus Points. (Count Occupations plus Improvements and exclude Begging cards.)

Name: Hunter
Card Text: Whenever you receive animals outside the Breeding phase of a Harvest, you may immediately convert them into Food. Take 2/3/4 Food for each Sheep/Wild Boar/Cattle converted.

Name: Huntsman’s Rifle
Card Text: Whenever you receive Wild boar outside the Breeding phase of a Harvest, you may immediately convert them into 4 Food each.

Name: Hut Builder
Card Text: Play this card during Round 1-4 and at the start of Round 11 you can extend your hut by 1 room at no cost, as long as you have not turned it into a stone house.

Name: Ice Cooler
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 3 food. After playing this card, the next player to have a Stone house (who currently doesn’t have a Stone house) receives 2 Bonus points.

Name: Immigrants Son
Card Text: When you play your 5th, 6th and 7th Occupation, you can Plow 1 field immediately afterward. (You cannot Plow immediately if you play this card as your 5th, 6th, or 7th Occupation.)

Name: Imported Timber
Card Text: When you play this card, you may immediately take a ”Fences” action and pay 1 Wood less for each completed Round of the game.

Name: Indentured Servant
Card Text: Indentured Servant Once any player has renovated to a Stone house, place 2 Food on each remaining Round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food. (If a player has already renovated to Stone, place the Food immediately.)

Name: Interior Design
Card Text: Whenever you play another Improvement, you may pay 1 additional building resource of a type in the printed cost to receive 3 Food.

Name: Inventor
Card Text: All improvements with a printed cost of 4 or more resources cost you 1 less resource of your choice.

Name: Inventory Manager
Card Text: Whenever you build an Improvement, you receive your choice of 1 Wood or Clay immediately after paying its cost.

Name: Investments
Card Text: You immediately receive 1 building resource of each type in which you have 5 or more building resources of that type in your supply. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Iron Oven
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Bake bread” action, you can use the Iron Oven to convert 1 Grain into 6 Food. When you play this card, you may immediately take a ”Bake bread” action.

Name: Iron Plow
Card Text: Once during the game, when you use the ”Plow 1 field” Action space, you may Plow 2 fields instead and take a ”Sow” action afterward.

Name: Irrigation Canal
Card Text: Whenever you plow a field that is orthogonally adjacent to a planted field, you may take 1 grain or vegetable from that field and sow it immediately on the newly plowed field. Place 1 fewer grain or vegetable from the supply on the newly planted field.

Name: Itinerant Laborers
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately take a ”Sow” action.

Name: Jeweler
Card Text: In the Field phase of each Harvest you may exchange exactly 2 stone for 1 Bonus Point and 3 Food.

Name: Job Seeker
Card Text: Once in play, this card does not count as an occupation for any purpose. Whenever, at the start of a round, you have fewer occupations than any 2 other players, you may pay 1 food to immediately play an occupation.

Name: Joiner
Card Text: Whenever you use a Family member’s action to take Food, you receive an additional 1 Wood.

Name: Journeyman
Card Text: Once you have a Stone house, you can pay 1 Food at the start of each round for 1 animal of your choice.

Name: Juggler
Card Text: Whenever you use the Travelling Players action on an Action space, you can take twice as much food as is on the card.

Name: Jungle
Card Text: At the start of each Round, add 1 Food to one of the Wood accumulation spaces with the fewest Wood.

Name: Junior Partner
Card Text: Whenever any player (including you) plays a Passing card, you receive 2 food.

Name: Junk Dealer
Card Text: You receive 1 Clay/1 Reed whenever you use a Food/Animal accumulation space.

Name: Kelp Gatherer
Card Text: Whenever another player uses the ”Fishing” Action space, they receive 1 additional Food and you receive 1 Vegetable.

Name: Keys
Card Text: You can use the ”Build room(s) and/or Build stable(s)” Action space, even if another player has already placed a person on it.

Name: Khnum
Card Text: When you play this card, if you have at least 4/8 Sheep on your farm, you may immediately take a ”Family Growth with room” action/ ”Family Growth without room” action.

Name: Kid’s Corner
Card Text: Your home may hold 1 additional animal. You may keep more than 1 type of animal in your home.

Name: Kindling
Card Text: You may build Major Improvement Fireplace and Cooking Hearths with a ”Minor Improvement” action. Pay 1 Clay less for each.

Name: Kitchen Cabinet
Card Text: Whenever you select the ”Minor Improvement” action onan Action space, you can play a Major instead of a Minor Improvement.

Name: Kitchen Table
Card Text: For you, all ”Bake Bread” actions now read ”Bake Bread or place 1 Food from your supply on Kitchen Table.” At any time, you may return 1 Food from this card to your supply and take a ”Bake Bread” Action.

Name: Kosher Butcher
Card Text: You may not convert Wild boar to Food. For every Sheep or Cattle you convert to Food, you receive 1 additional Food.

Name: Kudzu
Card Text: Place 1 Reed from the supply on 1 of your unused farmyard spaces. At the start of each round, you must place 1 Reed from the supply on another unused space orthogonally adjacent to a previously placed Reed (if able). These spaces count as used. (You may not remove the Reed or use the spaces.)

Name: Ladder
Card Text: You need 1 less reed to extend or renovate your home or to build the Water Mill I103, Half-timbered House E21, Chicken Coop I84, Holiday House I71, Mansion K144, or Corn Storehouse I86.

Name: Lady in Waiting
Card Text: If you play this card during Stage 1, place 1 person still in your supply on Round 12. At the start of this round, you can pay 2 Food to move this person into your home whether or not you have room. It counts as an adult Family member and may take actions starting Round 12.

Name: Lady of the Manor
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 Wood. At the end of the game, any player who scored the maximum 4 points in the most scoring categories receives 3 Bonus points. (The scoring categories include 4 fenced stables.)

Name: Lamp Oil
Card Text: Whenever you play an Occupation, you receive 1 Food before you pay the cost of the Occupation.

Name: Land Agent
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Take 1 vegetable' Action space, also take 1 grain. When you play this card, take 1 vegetable from the supply.

Name: Landing Net
Card Text: Whenever you receive reed on an action space, you receive an additional 2 food. This is reduced to 1 food if you receive other building resources as well as reed.

Name: Landscape Gardener
Card Text: When you Sow, you may Sow on this card as if it were 2 fields. When you play this card, you may take a ”Sow” action. (This card does not count as a field when scoring.)

Name: Land Surveyor
Card Text: In the Field phase of each Harvest, you receive 1/2/3/4 Food for having 2/4/6/7 Plowed fields.

Name: Large Sunhat
Card Text: At the start of the returning home phases of rounds 3 and 6, you can move your person from the ”Plow 1 field” action space to an unoccupied action space and take that action. If you do not occupy ”Plow 1 field”, you receive 1 food.

Name: Lasso
Card Text: You can place exactly two people immediately after one another, if at least one of them uses the ”1 Wild Boar”, ”1 Cattle” or ”1 Sheep” action space.

Name: Late Bloomer
Card Text: Whenever you have fewer family members than all of the other players and you have room in your home, you may immediately take a ”family growth” action. You need room in your home for the offspring. Place the offspring in an empty room. This family member is not considered a newborn and may take an action the same round.

Name: Late Sleeper
Card Text: At the start of each round, announce whether or not you will sleep late. If you sleep late, you place all of your people after everyone else has placed all of theirs, but you may also place a guest token.

Name: Layabout
Card Text: Once you have played this card, you may not take part in the next Harvest. (You do not need to feed your family during that Harvest.)

Name: Leftovers
Card Text: After the Feeding phase, you may put 1 Food from your supply to a farmyard space within a pasture. You can have a maximum of 1 Food for each pasture. At the end of the game, each Food is worth 1 Bonus point.

Name: Lemon Trader
Card Text: Twice per round, you may exchange 1 Grain from your supply for 1 building resource, or 1 Vegetable from your supply for 2 different building resources.

Name: Lettuce Patch
Card Text: On this card, you can plant vegetables as you would on a field. Vegetables on this field can be converted to 4 food when harvested.

Name: Lighthouse
Card Text: The Lighthouse provides room for 1 Family member.

Name: Liquid Manure
Card Text: Whenever you sow, place 1 additional grain or vegetable from the general supply on each new stack you sow.

Name: Livestock Expert
Card Text: If you play this card in Round 11 or before, you immediately double the amount of sheep, wild boar or cattle on your farm. (Take the additional animals from the supply.)

Name: Livestock Keeper
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 1 Wild boar. If you already have a Wild boar, you receive 1 Cattle instead. In the Breeding phase of each Harvest, you may pay 1 Food to receive 2 offsprings of exactly 1 animal type that breeds instead of 1.

Name: Longbowman
Card Text: Whenever you use an Action space orthogonally adjacent to an animal Accumulation space, you may immediately afterward place 1 Wood from your supply on the animal Accumulation space and take 1 of the animals. The next player to use that space receives the Wood.

Name: Loom
Card Text: Whenever you have sheep during the field phase of a harvest, you receive food: 1 food if you have 1 to 3 sheep; 2 food for 4 to 6 sheep; or 3 food for 7 or more sheep. At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for every 3 sheep.

Name: Lord of the Manor
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point in each scoring category where you have scored the maximum 4 points. (The bonus point is also awarded for 4 fenced stables.)

Name: Lorelei
Card Text: Whenever any player uses the ”Fishing” Action space, you may pay that player 1 Food to receive 1 Grain.

Name: Lover
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately carry out a 'Family growth even without room in the hut or house' action.

Name: Lumber
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 3 wood. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Lumber Pile
Card Text: When you play this card, you can return up to 3 stables from your farmyard to your supply and receive 3 Wood for each.

Name: Machete
Card Text: During the Field phase of each Harvest you may harvest all the goods planted in 1 of your fields.

Name: Madonna Statue
Card Text: The Madonna Statue has no effect.

Name: Magic Beans
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 2 vegetables.

Name: Magician
Card Text: Whenever you use your last person to choose the 'Travelling Players' action on an Action space, take an additional 1 grain and 1 food.

Name: Magnet
Card Text: At the end of each round, if you have no Stone in your supply, you may take 1 Stone from an accumulating Stone Action space.

Name: Maid
Card Text: Once you have built a Clay hut, place 1 food on each remaining Round space. At the start of these rounds, take the food.

Name: Mailman
Card Text: Place 2 Wood, 2 Clay, 1 Reed, and 1 Stone on four different revealed Action spaces respectively (occupied or unoccupied.) Afterward, any player (including you) who uses one of these spaces, may also take the extra resource(s) by paying you 1 Food first.

Name: Manger
Card Text: At the end of the game, if your pastures occupy 6/7/8/9+ farmyard spaces, you receive 1/2/3/4 bonus points.

Name: Mangrove Tree
Card Text: Whenever you use a Person to take either the Take 1 Reed” accumulation action space or the ”Fishing” action space, you receive 1 Wood from the supply.

Name: Manservant
Card Text: When you build a Stone house, place 3 food on each remaining Round space. At the start of these rounds, take the food.

Name: Mansion
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 2 bonus points for each room in your stone house. In total, you receive 4 points per room instead of the usual 2 points.

Name: Manual Labourer
Card Text: At the end of each round in which you received exactly 1 type of building resource (in any way), you also receive 1 more building resource of that type.

Name: Manufacturer
Card Text: Once you have a Clay hut or a Stone house, the Cabinetmaker's, Pottery and Basket Weaver's are Minor improvements for you and their cost is reduced by 2 resources of your choice.

Name: Manure
Card Text: At the end of each round that does not end with a harvest, you can take 1 grain or vegetable from each of your fields and place it in your personal supply.

Name: Manure Shoveler
Card Text: When you have 2/4/6+ Animals you receive one Grain/Vegetable/Grain and Vegetable before you take a Sow action.

Name: March
Card Text: When you play this card, you may return 2 different animals to the general supply to Plow 2 fields.

Name: Margrave
Card Text: If you live in a Stone house, you receive 2 Food whenever a player renovates and you receive 1 Bonus point at the end of the game for every opponent who does not live in a Stone house.

Name: Market
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 1 Grain, 1 Vegetable and 2 Food.

Name: Market Crier
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Take 1 grain' Action space, you can take an additional 1 grain and 1 vegetables. If you do this, the other players each receive 1 grain from the supply.

Name: Market Stall
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 1 vegetable. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left.

Name: Martial Artist
Card Text: At the start of each Harvest, you may discard up to 2 Minor Improvements from your hand to receive 2 Food each.

Name: Mason
Card Text: Once during the game, at any time after your stone house reaches at least 4 rooms, you may extend it by 1 room at no cost.

Name: Mason’s Apprentice
Card Text: Once you have a Stone house, at the start of each round, you can pay 3 Stone and 2 Reed to build a room without placing a Family member.

Name: Master Baker
Card Text: Whenever another player bakes, you can bake if you have a Baking improvement. If you take a Bake action yourself (not using this card), take 1 additional food.

Name: Master Baker’s Apprentice
Card Text: When you play this card, mark a revealed action space with an arrow marker. This action space now has the added text ”and/or bake bread”. Whenever another player bakes bread using this space, you receive 1 food from the general supply. When you play this card, you may also take the ”Bake bread” action.

Name: Master Brewer
Card Text: In the Feeding phase of each Harvest, the Master Brewer can convert up to 1 grain to 3 food.

Name: Master Builder
Card Text: Once during the game, at any time after your home reaches at least 5 rooms, you may extend it by 1 room at no cost.

Name: Master Builder
Card Text: Once during the game, at any time after your home reaches at least 5 rooms, you may extend it by 1 room at no cost.

Name: Master Cook
Card Text: Each round, instead of placing your first Family member on an Action space, you may place it on this card and receive 2 Food and 1 Guest token to place that round. (You may not do this if you will be placing more people after your first without another player placing in between.)

Name: Master Forester
Card Text: Include the '2 Wood' Action card from the 3-player game as an additional forest. At the start of each round, place 2 wood on the card. Any player who uses this action must pay you 2 food.

Name: Master Huntsman
Card Text: Whenever you build a Major Improvement, you receive 1 Wild boar. When you play this card, you receive 1 Wild boar.

Name: Mastermind
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus Point for each card with a Bonus point symbol that you play after this one. (An example of a Bonus point symbol appears on the bottom of this card.)

Name: Master Oven
Card Text: Whenever you use a Bread baking action, you can use the Master Oven to convert up to 2 Grain into 5 Food each. When you play this card, you can immediately take the ”Bake bread” action.

Name: Master Renovator
Card Text: At the end of the Work phases in rounds 7 and 9, you may take a ”Renovation” action without placing a person and pay 1 less building resource.

Name: Master Shepherd
Card Text: Place 1 sheep on each of the next 3 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, take the sheep.

Name: Master Stableman
Card Text: Whenever you use a person’s action to take 2 or more animals from an Accumulation space, you can also build up to 2 stables, at a cost of 1 Wood each, before taking the animals.

Name: Master Tanner
Card Text: Whenever you convert Wild boar or Cattle into Food, you may place 1 Food from your supply on this card for each animal. Once during the game, when there is Food on this card equal to the number of your rooms, play your next ”Family growth” action as ”Family growth even without room”.

Name: Mathematician
Card Text: From round 6 on, if there are wood spaces with the same amount of wood after refilling each round (at the end of phase 2), you can take 1 wood from one of those spaces. This also applies to clay action spaces. In 5-player game, this also applies to stone action spaces. You can take only 1 resource per round.

Name: Mattock
Card Text: Whenever you receive Reed or Stone from an Action space, you receive 1 additional Clay.

Name: Mayor Candidate
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 2 Wood and 2 Stone. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Negative point for each Wood and each Stone in your supply. (You can’t return building resources to the supply without reason.)

Name: Mbaba Mwana
Card Text: When you Plow your 4th / 7th Field, you immediately receive a ”Family Growth with room” action / ”Family Growth without room” action.

Name: Meadow
Card Text: The edge of your farm now forms a natural border for pastures. (This does not count as building fences or taking a ”Fences” action.)

Name: Meat Pie
Card Text: Whenever you bake 1 or more grain into bread, you may pay 1 additional grain and 1 animal for 3 Bonus points. When you play this card, you may also take the ”Bake bread” action.

Name: Meat Seller
Card Text: If you have an Oven, you can change your animals into food at any time. Take 2 food for each sheep, 3 for each wild boar and 4 for each cattle marker.

Name: Melon Patch
Card Text: When you Sow, you can plant Vegetables on this card as though it were field. When you harvest the last Vegetable on this card, you may Plow 1 field. (This card does nor count as field when scoring.)

Name: Menagerie
Card Text: Whenever you receive an animal of a type you don’t have on your farm, you receive 1 Food.

Name: Mendicant
Card Text: At the end of the game, you can discard up to 2 Begging cards without losing points for them.

Name: Merchant
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Minor Improvement' or 'Minor or Major Improvement' action, you can pay 1 food to use the action a second time.

Name: Merchant Plowman
Card Text: The Joinery is a now a Minor Improvement for you. Whenever you use the Joinery, Sawmill, or Cabinetmaker to convert 1 or more Wood to Food during the Harvest, you may pay 1 Food to Plow 1 field.

Name: Meticulous Men
Card Text: Place 8 Food from the supply on this card. Each time your Family members take exactly 2 goods from an Accumulation space, you also take 1 Food from this card. You score 2 Bonus points if there is no Food on this card at the end of the game.

Name: Midnight Fencer
Card Text: At the start of the final Harvest, you may take up to 2 of each other player’s unused fences and build them on your farm without paying Wood. (After you do this, your farm may have more than 15 built fences.) Native (tmp4) Whenever you use the ”Plow 1 Field” or ”Sow and/ or Bake Bread” Action spaces, you receive 1 Reed.

Name: Midwife
Card Text: Whenever another player has a larger family than you after having offspring, you receive 1 food. If s/he has at least 2 more family members than you, you receive 2 food.

Name: Migrant Workers
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Day Labourer” action space, you may immediately afterward pay 1 food to plow 1 field.

Name: Milkcan
Card Text: You immediately receive 2 Food for each different type of animal on your farmyard. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Milking Hand
Card Text: In the Field phase of the Harvest, you receive 1/2/3 food for 1/3/5 cows, without having to give up the cows. At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for every 2 cows.

Name: Milking Shed
Card Text: In each harvest, at the beginning of the field phase, count the total number of sheep and cattle in all the players’ farms. You receive 1 food for each fifth sheep and for each third cattle.

Name: Milking Stool
Card Text: Whenever you have cattle during the field phase of a harvest, you receive food: 1 food if you have 1 or 2 cattle; 2 food if you have 3 or 4 cattle; 3 food if you have 5 or more cattle. At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for every 2 cattle.

Name: Miller
Card Text: Whenever another player uses the ”Take 1 Grain” Action space, you may Bake bread. When you play this card, you can build a Baking Improvement by paying its costs.

Name: Millstone
Card Text: Whenever you bake 1 or more loaves of bread, take 2 additional food.

Name: Miner
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Day Labourer” action space, you can take all the stone markers from one of the ”1 Stone” action spaces (stage 2 or stage 4) in the returning home phase (phase 4).

Name: Minimalist
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 9/6/4/2 Bonus points for having exactly 0/1/2/3 Improvements in front of you.

Name: Mini Pasture
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately fence one space in your farmyard. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left.

Name: Miser
Card Text: Whenever you use a ”Build Room(s)” action on an Action space, you may pay 1 Wood/ Clay/ Stone less and 1 Reed less if you build exactly 1 Wood/ Clay/ Stone room. (It is irrelevant whether you build stables.)

Name: Mooch
Card Text: When you play this card, choose another player. Until the end of the next harvest, you may convert animals, vegetables, grain, and building resources to food as if you owned the chosen player’s improvements in addition to yours.

Name: Mountain Cave
Card Text: Each Harvest, you may use the Mountain Cave to exchange up to 1 Wood for 1 Stone.

Name: Mountain Plowman
Card Text: Whenever you Plow 1 or more fields, you receive 1 Sheep from the supply for each field you just plowed.

Name: Muddy Land
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Plow 1 field” Action space, you receive 2 Clay for each field you Plow.

Name: Muddy Waters
Card Text: Alternate placing 1 Food andr Clay on each remaining even-numbered Round space starting with Food. At the start of these Rounds, you receive the item.

Name: Mushroom Collector
Card Text: Whenever you use a family member's action to take wood from an Action space, you can leave 1 of the wood on the space and take 2 food in exchange.

Name: Musketeer
Card Text: Place an Arrow marker in the intersection between 4 Action spaces. At the end of each Work phase, if 2/3/4 of those spaces are occupied by your Family members, you receive 1 Sheep / Wild boar / Cattle.

Name: Nail Basket
Card Text: Whenever you use a Wood Accumulation space, you may place 1 Stone from your supply on the space to take a ”Fences” Action. The next player to use the space takes the Stone.

Name: Naturalist
Card Text: When you Plow your 5th, 6th, 7th, and 8th field, you may pay 1 Food to receive a Guest token that you place that round as you would a Family member.

Name: Necklace
Card Text: Whenever at the end of a Work phase, you have at least 2 Family members occupying 2 orthogonally adjacent Action spaces, you receive 1 Food.

Name: Net Fisherman
Card Text: If one of your people uses an Action space that provides reeds, you can take all food markers from the 'Catch fish' space in the Returning home phase.

Name: Night Bazaar
Card Text: Immediately take 1 building resource from 2 Accumulation spaces. The resources taken must be different types. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Ninja
Card Text: Immediately move the person that played this Occupation to an occupied Action space except the ”Starting Player” Action space, and take that action.

Name: Noble
Card Text: Once you live in a Stone house you may use an occupied Action space other than ”Start Player and/or Minor Improvement” once each round.

Name: Nurse Maid
Card Text: Whenever another player has a larger family than you after offspring, you receive 1 Food. If that player has at least 2 more Family members than you, you receive 1 Good of your choice instead.

Name: Oak Cask
Card Text: When you play this card, place 1 Food from the general supply on it. During the Field phase of each Harvest, add 1 Food from the general supply to this card if it has Food on it. At any time, you may take all of the Food from this card.

Name: Oceanographer
Card Text: Pile (from bottom to top) 1 Vegetable, 1 Stone, 1 Grain, 1 Clay, 1 Wood on this card. Whenever you Plow exactly 1 field, you may receive the top item.

Name: Officer
Card Text: Once you live in a Stone house, you may pay 1 Food at the start of each round for a Guest token which you must place that round.

Name: Oldest Occupation
Card Text: From round 5 on, whenever any player uses the ”Day Labourer” action space, they may pay you 2 food to immediately take a ”family growth” action.

Name: Old Hiker
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 1 building resource of your choice and a Guest token which you may place this round after all players have placed all of their Family members.

Name: Old Miser
Card Text: In the Feeding phase of each Harvest, each of your Family members (including newborns) requires 1 Food less. At the end of the game, you only score 2 Bonus points for each Family member (instead of 3).

Name: Omnifarmer
Card Text: Each harvest, you can either place 1 harvested crop or 1 newborn animal on this card. You can no longer use the goods on this card. During the scoring, you receive 3/s/7/9 bonus points for 2/3/4/5 different goods on this card.

Name: Open Fields
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 2 Bonus points if you have 4 or fewer built fences.

Name: Open Gate
Card Text: At the start of the Feeding phase of each Harvest, you may return 1 Sheep to the general supply to receive 1 Bonus point.

Name: Opportunist
Card Text: Whenever you use your last action in a round to take one or more types of building resources that have not yet been taken this work phase, you receive 1 additional resource of one of those types.

Name: Orchard
Card Text: Place 1 Food on each of the next Round spaces up to the number of planted fields in your farmyard. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.

Name: Organic Farmer
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each pasture that contains at least 1 animal, but could contain at least 3 more animals.

Name: Organised Handyman
Card Text: Place up to 1 Minor Improvement (from your hand) face down on each space for rounds 9, 11, and 13. Ar the start of these rounds, you may play the Improvement by paying its cost. If you do, you receive 2 Food. If you do not, return it to your hand.

Name: Outhouse
Card Text: The Outhouse has no effect. You can only build it if at least one other player has fewer than 2 occupations.

Name: Outrider
Card Text: Whenever you use the most recent Round card with one of your people, take 1 additional grain.

Name: Oven Admirer
Card Text: When you play this card, if you have the Stone Oven, you receive 2 Vegetables and 1 Animal of your choice.

Name: Oven Inspector
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Bake bread” action, you can use this card to convert up to 1 Grain into 4 Food. When you play this card, you can also take the ”Bake bread” action. If another player builds an Oven, you may immediately pay 1 Food to play this Occupation.

Name: Over Achiever
Card Text: Whenever you use a ”Family growth” Action, you may play 1 additional Major or Minor Improvement by paying its cost less 1 resource of your choice.

Name: Overgrown Pond
Card Text: Alternate placing 1 Wood and 1 Food on the next 4 Round spaces. Start with the item of your choice. At the start of these rounds, you receive the item.

Name: Ox Goad
Card Text: Whenever you receive Cattle from an Action space, you may pay 2 Food to Plow 1 field.

Name: Ox Skull
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 3 Bonus points if you have no Cattle.

Name: Ox Team
Card Text: When you play this card, count how many complete rounds are left to be played. You can plough this many fields, up to a maximum of 3.

Name: Oyster Recipe
Card Text: Whenever you take the ”Fishing” Action space, you may have one pair of animals on your farmyard breed.

Name: Paddocks
Card Text: You may not use the Paddocks to fence unfenced farmyard spaces.

Name: Paintbrush
Card Text: Each Harvest, you may convert 1 Clay into 2 Food or 1 Bonus point.

Name: Paleontologist
Card Text: On this card you can hold 2 Animals of your choice for every complete harvest remaining.

Name: Panda
Card Text: From now on, one of your pastures containing a stable may not hold animals. Whenever you take Reed from an Action space, you receive 1 Bonus point.

Name: Paper Oven
Card Text: Whenever you take the ”Bake bread” action, you can pay 1 Reed to use the Paper Oven to turn exactly 1 Grain into 6 food. When you play this card, you can immediately take the ”Bake bread” action.

Name: Parchment
Card Text: At the start of each round, you may pay 1 Sheep to play 1 Occupation.

Name: Par Force Hunting
Card Text: Add 4 and 7 to the current round and place 1 Wild boar on each corresponding Round space. At the start of these rounds, you can pay 1 Food to take the Wild boar.

Name: Parquet Setter
Card Text: At the end of each Work phase in which you reduced the number of your unused farmyard spaces, you receive 1 Wood. If you reduced it by 2 or more spaces, you also receive 1 Food.

Name: Part Time Builder
Card Text: When you play this card, if you have 4 stables in your supply, place 1 stable on each of the ”Take 1 Grain”, ”Plow 1 field” , ”Day Laborer”, and ”Build rooms” Action spaces. When another player uses the space, build the stable on your farm for free. You may not use a space with one of your stables on it.

Name: Parvenu
Card Text: If you play an ad in Round 7 or before, you immediately double the amount of Clay or Reed in your supply.

Name: Pasteurization Expert
Card Text: Pile (from bottom to top) 1 Cattle, Sheep, Wild boar, Sheep on this card. You receive the top item when you receive that type of animal outside of the Breeding phase of Harvest.

Name: Pastor
Card Text: If, when you play this card or later in the game, you are the last player to only have 2 rooms in your home, you receive 3 Wood, 2 Clay, 1 Reeds and 1 Stone.

Name: Pastureland
Card Text: Place 1 Sheep, 1 Wild boar and 1 Cattle on the next 3 Round spaces in this order. At the start of these rounds’ Work phase, place the animal on an Action space of your choice. (The next player to use this space receives the animal.)

Name: Pasture Lover
Card Text: If there are 2/3/5 pastures on your farm, you may pay 2/3/4 fewer building resources of your choice for each new Wooden room.

Name: Pasture Planner
Card Text: If played in Stage 1, place 1 Wood from the general supply on as many unused Farmyard spaces as you choose. These spaces can only be used for pastures. You may use the Wood to pay for a pasture on that space. You may not build pastures on spaces without Wood.

Name: Patron
Card Text: In future, whenever you play an occupation, take 2 food before you pay the costs of the occupation.

Name: Patroness
Card Text: Whenever you play an Occupation after this one, you receive 1 building resource of your choice afterward.

Name: Paved Road
Card Text: The player with the most valuable street receives 2 Bonus points when scoring.

Name: Pawnbroker
Card Text: At any time, you may convert all the printed Victory points (if any) of an Improvement in front of you to 3 Food per point. Afterward, you may not return or remove the Improvement and you no longer receive its Victory points. If the Pawnbroker gets turned face down or discarded, you still lose the points.

Name: Pearl Seeker
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Fishing” action space you receive 1 additional stone. Also, you can use food that you take from that action space to buy more stone. Each stone costs 2 food.

Name: Pear Peeler
Card Text: Whenever you use an action to take Wood from an Action space, you may leave 1/2/3 Wood on the space and take 1 Grain/ Vegetable/ Grain and Vegetable in exchange.

Name: Peasant
Card Text: Whenever you build a Major improvement, you may pay 1 Food to Plow 1 field afterward.

Name: Peasants Boutique
Card Text: Every harvest you may convert 1 Reed into 4 Food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 Bonus points for having 1/3/4 Reed. You do not receive additional Bonus points from the Basket Maker’s Workshop.

Name: Pelt Roof
Card Text: For each wild boar, cattle, or 2 sheep you convert into food, you may place 1 reed from the general supply on this card. You may use this reed to extend or renovate your home.

Name: Pelts
Card Text: For each animal that you convert to food and return to the general supply, you may place 1 food from your personal supply in 1 of your rooms. You may have a maximum of 1 food in each room. You cannot use these food any more, but each is worth 1 bonus point at the end of the game.

Name: Pen Builder
Card Text: At any time, you may place Wood on this card from your supply. You may not use this Wood for anything. For each Wood on this card you may hold 2 animals of any type on this card.

Name: Peon
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Take 1 Grain” Action space, you may take a ”Bake bread” action afterward. Whenever you use the ”Take 1 Vegetable” Action space, you may take a ”Sow” action afterward.

Name: Permaculturist
Card Text: You may sow Grain on Unused farmyard spaces. Planted spaces count as Grain fields and do not need to be adjacent to your existing plowed fields. You may move Grain from these fields to the general supply at any time and you must move Grain before scoring. (Or if this card gets removed or turned face down).

Name: Perpetual Student
Card Text: Whenever you can play an occupation card, you can ask an other player to randomly draw one of your occupation cards instead of choosing one yourself. If you do this, you receive 3 food before you pay the costs of the card, but you must play the card that the other player draws.

Name: Pet Door
Card Text: As long as you have a pasture orthogonally adjacent to your home, you may keep animals of any type on this card up to the number of rooms in your home.

Name: Petting Zoo
Card Text: At the end of the game, if you have all the animal types in your farm, you receive 1 Bonus point if you have at least 2 Sheep and 1 Bonus point if you have at least 2 Wild boar.

Name: Photographer
Card Text: Whenever you take a Family Growth or Renovation action, place 1 Food from the general supply on this card. You cannot use these Food, but each is worth 1 Bonus point at the end of the game.

Name: Pickler
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 Wood. At the end of the game, all players who have the most Vegetables receive 3 Bonus points each.

Name: Pieceworker
Card Text: Whenever you receive wood, clay, reed, stone or grain on an action space, you can buy one more of the same good for 1 food. Whenever you receive vegetable(s) on an action space, you can buy one more for 2 food.

Name: Pig Breeder
Card Text: Your wild boar also breed at the end of Round 12, if there is place for the piglet. When you play this card, take 1 wild boar.

Name: Pig Catcher
Card Text: Whenever you use a person to take wood that is on an Action space, you can leave 2 of the wood on the space and take a wild boar instead.

Name: Piggy Bank
Card Text: At the end of each Work Phase, you may place 1 Food from your supply on Piggy Bank. You cannot use this Food anymore. At any time, you may discard 6 Food on Piggy Bank to build a Major Improvement for free.

Name: Pig Wallow
Card Text: Immediately return 1 empty field to the general supply to fence a single farmyard space (in the same or different space.) (Normal fencing rules still apply.) You do not need to pay Wood for the fences. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Pig Whisperer
Card Text: Add 4, 7 and 10 to the current round and place 1 wild boar on each corresponding Round space. At the start of these rounds, take the wild boar.

Name: Pilgrim
Card Text: You receive 2 food before you pay the costs of playing this occupation. At the start of a round, if you have no building resources, you may take 1 building resource from the general supply; if you have no food, you may take 1 food from the general supply.

Name: Pine Forest
Card Text: At the start of each round, you may place 1 wood on this card if you have zero wood in your supply. When this card has 5 wood, move the wood from this card to your personal supply.

Name: Pioneer
Card Text: Whenever you use the most recent Round card with one of your people, you receive an additional 1 building resource of your choice and 1 Food before taking the action.

Name: Pipe Smoker
Card Text: Whenever you have at least 1 planted Grain field at the start of a Harvest, you receive 2 Wood.

Name: Plane
Card Text: Whenever you turn 1 wood into food using the Cabinetmaker's, Sawmill or Cabinetmaker, you receive 1 additional food. You can choose instead to turn a second wood into exactly 2 food.

Name: Planter Box
Card Text: Whenever you sow, each of the fields you sow that is orthogonally adjacent to a room in your home gets additional goods from the general supply: 2 extra grain on grain fields, and 1 extra vegetable on vegetable fields.

Name: Plentiful Fields
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 1 food for each planted plowed field in your farmyard. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Plow Deal Maker
Card Text: Whenever another player uses the ”Plow 1 Field” Action space, you can pay that player 2 food to Plow 1 Field yourself without placing a Family member.

Name: Plow Feast
Card Text: When you play this card, each player may immediately pay 1 Food to the general supply and place 1 Food on the ”1 Grain” action space, to Plow 1 Field.

Name: Plowman’s Lunch
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Bake Bread” Action, you may pay 1 Food to Plow 1 Field.

Name: Plum Patch
Card Text: Place 1 Food on each of the next Round spaces, up to the number of planted fields in your farmyard. At the start of those rounds, you receive the food.

Name: Poker Player
Card Text: If this is your first Occupation, you receive 9/6/5/3 Bonus points at the end of the game if you played 7 Occupations by the end of round 8/10/12/14.

Name: Pole Barns
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately build up to 3 stables at No cost. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Politician
Card Text: Twice each round, you may exchange 1 Reed for 1 Stone, or 1 Stone for 1 Reed. When you play this card, you receive 1 Stone.

Name: Polymorph Master
Card Text: When you play this card, you may convert 1 Wood into 1 Sheep, up to 6 times.

Name: Pond Watchman
Card Text: At the end of a work phase, after all other players have taken their actions, you may pay 1 food to move one of your people that used the ”Fishing” action this round to an unoccupied wood accumulating action space and take that action.

Name: Popcorn
Card Text: At any time, you may convert 1 Reed into 1 Grain.

Name: Postbox
Card Text: Whenever you play a passing card you receive 1 Bonus point. Unassigned

Name: Potato Dibber
Card Text: Whenever you sow fields with vegetables, place 1 additional vegetable on each newly sown field.

Name: Potatoes
Card Text: Place 1 Food on the next 3 Round spaces. At the start of these Rounds, you receive the Food.

Name: Potter
Card Text: In each Harvest, the Potter can convert up to 1 clay to 2 food.

Name: Potter’s Wheel
Card Text: Whenever you convert 1 clay into food using the Pottery A8 or Potter E214, you receive 1 additional food. The Pottery is now a minor improvement for you.

Name: Powder Keg
Card Text: Place 3 Stone from the supply on this card. You may use this Stone only for renovations and only before the end of Round 11.

Name: Powerhouse
Card Text: Whenever you have at least 3/5 Stone in your supply at the start of the Work phase, you receive 1/2 Food.

Name: Prefect
Card Text: You receive 1 Food for each Occupation and for each Improvement used by any player to convert building resources to Food during Harvest.

Name: Primitive Man
Card Text: You receive 1 Wood at the start of each round, if you have no Improvement in front of you. From Rounds 6/10 on, you receive 1 building resource/1 good of your choice.

Name: Private Forest
Card Text: Place 1 wood on each remaining even-numbered Round space. At the start of these rounds, take the wood.

Name: Private Market
Card Text: Once per round, you may exchange 2 food for a building resource of your choice.

Name: Private Oven
Card Text: Whenever you take the ”Bake bread” action, you can use the Private Oven to turn exactly 1 grain into 5 food.When you play this card, you can immediately take the ”Bake bread” action.

Name: Private School
Card Text: This card is an additional action space for all players. Any player who uses the Private School must pay you 1 food to play an occupation. All players must still pay any additional costs for their occupation (for Chief E172, Lover K291, etc).

Name: Prized Squash
Card Text: For each Vegetable you convert to Food, you may place 1 of the Food on this card. You may not have more Food on this card than your number of plowed fields. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each Food on this card.

Name: Prize-winning Sheep
Card Text: You immediately receive 1 Building resource of your choice. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Probiotic Food
Card Text: When paying a cost, you may discard this card and pay 2 less Building resources of your choice.

Name: Project Servant
Card Text: You receive 3 Food if you have 1 Built Stable/1 Pasture/2 Sown Fields/Renovated to Stone at the end of working phase in Round 7/9/11/13.

Name: Prophet
Card Text: When you play this card, you may immediately perform the ”After Renovation, also Fences” action. (Pay costs normally.)

Name: Prosecutor
Card Text: If you have fewer Improvements than 2/3/4 other players, you may pay 1/2/3 building resource less of your choice when you play an Improvement.

Name: Prudent Merchant
Card Text: Once this game, immediatly after playing an Improvement, you may choose to receive its printed cost from the supply.

Name: Public Forest
Card Text: Place 1 Food on each remaining even-numbered Round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.

Name: Pulley
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Day Labourer” action space you may immediately afterward build one room.

Name: Pumpkin Pie
Card Text: Whenever you bake 1 or more grain into bread, you may pay 1 additional grain and 1 vegetable for 3 bonus points. When you play this card, you can also take the ”Bake bread” action.

Name: Pumpkin Trellis
Card Text: You may immediately take a ”Build fences” action. If there are 1/2/3+ complete rounds left to be played, you may build 3/6/9 fences at no cost.

Name: Punner
Card Text: Whenever another player uses the Harrow I68 or a plough, you can immediately plough 1 field as well.

Name: Puppeteer
Card Text: Whenever another player chooses the 'Travelling players' action on an Action space, you can pay 1 food to play an occupation.

Name: Quarry
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Day Labourer' Action space, you receive an additional 3 stone.

Name: Quarryman
Card Text: You can use the Quarryman at any time to convert stone to food. For each stone you convert, take 2 food.

Name: Racing Stable Manager
Card Text: Whenever you build at least 1 stable, you may also pay 1 Food to immediately Plow 1 field.

Name: Raft
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Catch Fish' Action space, take an additional 1 food or 1 reeds.

Name: Rake
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 2 bonus points if you have at least 5 fields. If you have played the Harrow I68, Punner I70, Yoke K124 or a plough, you need to have at least 6 fields.

Name: Ram
Card Text: The Ram counts as a Sheep in your farm for scoring and breeding, but must be fed 1 Food in the Feeding phase of each Harvest or it is discarded. Your sheep breed at the end of Rounds 6, 8, 10, and 12.

Name: Ram Wrangler
Card Text: Whenever you breed a Sheep, you receive 1 Grain afterward.

Name: Rancher
Card Text: Whenever, at the start of a round, you are using more farmyard spaces than every other player, you receive 1 wood.

Name: Raspberries
Card Text: Place 1 Food each on up to 3 Unused farmyard spaces from the general supply. When the space is used, you receive the Food.

Name: Ratcatcher
Card Text: In rounds 10 and 12, all other players may not place 1 of their family's offspring (if they have any). This card may only be played until the end of Round 9.

Name: Real Estate Agent
Card Text: At the start of each Field phase, if no other player has fewer unused spaces than you, receive 2 Food. At the end of each Field phase, you may rearrange your rooms, stables, fences and fields into a new (legal) configuration.

Name: Reed Beds
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Plough 1 Field” action space, you receive 1 reed.

Name: Reed Buyer
Card Text: Whenever reed is taken for the first time in a round, you may give the player who takes it 1 food in exchange for 1 of the reed. The other player receives an additional 1 food from the general supply as compensation.

Name: Reed Collector
Card Text: Place 1 reeds on each of the next 4 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the reeds.

Name: Reed Exchange
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 2 reeds. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Reed Hut
Card Text: Place one family member token that you have not yet brought into the game onto this card, where it will live for the rest of the game. It can take actions and must be fed, but it is not worth any points while living in the Reed Hut.

Name: Reed Nursery
Card Text: You may Sow and harvest Reed in your fields as if it were Grain. At the end of the game, you may exchange 1/4/6/8 Reed from your supply or from your fields for 1/2/3/4 Bonus points.

Name: Reed Pond
Card Text: Place 1 Reeds each on the next 3 remaining Round spaces. At the start of each round, you receive the reeds.

Name: Reed Sale
Card Text: In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

Name: Reeve
Card Text: Take 1/2/3/4 wood if there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play. At the end of the game, players with the most played Occupation cards each receive 3 bonus points.

Name: Reformer
Card Text: You may keep any 1 animal on each of your played Occupation cards.

Name: Remodeler
Card Text: When you renovate your Wooden hut, you may first return 1 built stable to extend your hut by 1 Wooden room for free before paying the costs of the Renovation. (The stable can be rebuilt later.)

Name: Remodeling
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately return a stable from your farmyard to your supply and extend your wooden hut by 1 room. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Renaissance Man
Card Text: At the start of each Work phase, if you have at least 1 Wood, Clay, Reed, Stone, Grain, and Vegetable in your personal supply, you receive 1 Bonus point. (Planted resources and resources occupying farm spaces do not count.)

Name: Renovation Materials
Card Text: Immediately renovate to Clay for free. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Renovator
Card Text: Pay 2 less clay to renovate to a clay hut; 2 less stone to renovate to a stone house.

Name: Resource Collector
Card Text: Place 1 wood, 1 clay, 1 reed, and 1 stone on the next 4 round spaces in any order you choose. At the start of those rounds, you receive the resource.

Name: Resource Gatherer
Card Text: At the start of each round, if you live in a stone house, you may choose to receive 2 Wood, 2 Clay, 1 Reed, or 1 Stone from the general supply.

Name: Resource Lender
Card Text: Whenever you receive Building resources from an Action space, instead of placing them in your supply, you may add 1 resource of 1 type received and place them all on the next Round spaces with 1 per space (in any order and discard any excess.) At the start of these rounds, you receive the resource.

Name: Resource Seller
Card Text: Pile (from bottom to top) 1 Stone, Clay, Stone, Clay, Reeds, Clay, Wood on this card. Take the top marker when you receive that type of resource.

Name: Restless Father
Card Text: Whenever you take a ”Family growth” action, you can immediately Plow 1 field.

Name: Rice Farmer
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Take 1 Reed” Action space, you receive an additional 1 Grain. Whenever you use the ”Take 1 Grain” Action space, you receive an additional 1 Reed.

Name: Ringmaster
Card Text: When you use the ”Traveling Players” action, you may either play a minor improvement or you may pay 1 grain to build a major improvement immediately afterward.

Name: Riverman
Card Text: Whenever you use a Family members action to take the ”1 Reed” action space you receive an additional 2 Food or can pay 1 Food to receive an additional Reed.

Name: River Mill
Card Text: After the Field phase of a Harvest, each player may place 1 Grain from their supply on the River Mill to gain 4 Food. You cannot use these Grain, bur you receive 1 Bonus point for every 2 Grain on the card at the end of the game.

Name: River Pig
Card Text: At the start of each oddnumbered round, place 1 Wood from the general supply on the ”Fishing” Action space. At the end of the game you receive 2/3 Bonus points if you have 3/5 Wood in your supply.

Name: Rock Garden
Card Text: When you Sow, you can plant up to 3 Stone on this card. The Stone is treated the same as sown Vegetables and is harvested during the Field phase. (This card does not count as a Field when scoring.)

Name: Rock Pyramid
Card Text: Whenever you build a Stone room, you can Plow up to 1 field. If you paid at least 1 Stone for that room, you may Plow up to 2 fields instead.

Name: Rogue
Card Text: Once per round, you may use an occupied Action space other than ”Start Player and/or Minor Improvement” if you have no played Minor Improvements in front of you.

Name: Rooster
Card Text: You may play this card immediately before the Round 1 Work phase without taking an action. If you do, pay 1 Food to the player with the starting player token in exchange for it. At the end of the game, the player with the starting player token receives 1 Bonus point.

Name: Rope Fences
Card Text: Once during the game, when you build at least 1 fence, you may pay 2 Reed to build up to 8 additional fences at no cost.

Name: Rotherham Plow
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Plow 1 field” Action space, you may Plow 2 fields if they are not diagonally or orthogonally adjacent to each other instead of 1.

Name: Rotten Plow
Card Text: Whenever you use either the ”Plow 1 field” or ”Plow 1 field and/ or Sow” Action space, you can Plow 2 fields instead of 1. Whenever you Sow, place 1 fewer resource from the general supply on each field you Sow.

Name: Ruins
Card Text: Place 4 Stone on this card from the general supply. In the Field phase of each Harvest, you may move 1 Stone from this card to your supply.

Name: Rule Breaker
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each scoring category where you have scored exactly 3 points.

Name: Rum Distillery
Card Text: During the Feeding phase of each Harvest, you may use the Rum Distillery to convert at most 1 Grain into 3 Food and 1 Bonus point.

Name: Runestone
Card Text: At the end of Round 10 and 12 you may take a Field phase and a Breeding phase on your farm. This does not count as a Harvest.

Name: Sack Cart
Card Text: Place 1 grain each on the spaces for rounds 5, 8, 11 and 14. At the start of these rounds, take the grain.

Name: Sailboat Constructor
Card Text: Place 4 stacks of resources on this card: 3 Wood, 3 Clay, 2 Stone, and 1 Grain with 1 Vegetable. In the Field phase of each Harvest you can buy (some or all of) these stacks. Pay 2/3/4/5 Food for the 1st/ 2nd/ 3rd/ 4th stack you buy.

Name: Salary
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Day Laborer” Action space, you may pay any 1 building resource to receive 1 building resource of your choice. When you play this card, you receive 1 Food.

Name: Sapling
Card Text: Add 5 to the current Round number and place 6 Wood on the corresponding Round space. At the start of that Round you receive the Wood.

Name: Sausage Grinder
Card Text: Whenever you convert animals into food, you receive 1 additional food for each animal.

Name: Sawhorse
Card Text: The next stable you place in your farmyard, as well as your 3rd, 6th, 9th, 12th and 15th fence, costs you nothing.

Name: Sawmill
Card Text: Each harvest, you may convert up to 1 wood to 3 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 bonus points for 2/4/5 wood.

Name: Scapegrace
Card Text: When you play this, you receive 1 Bonus point for each complete Stage left to be played. You may not play Occupations anymore.

Name: Schnaps Distiller
Card Text: In the Feeding phase of each Harvest, the Schnaps Distiller can convert up to 1 vegetable to 5 food.

Name: Schnaps Distillery
Card Text: During the feeding phase of the harvest, you can use the Schnaps Distillery to convert at most 1 vegetable into 4 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point each for your 5th and 6th vegetables.

Name: Scholar
Card Text: Once you have a Stone house, at the start of a Round you can always either pay 1 Food to play an Occupation card or play an Improvement card by paying its costs.

Name: School Lunch Lady
Card Text: In the future, whenever you play an Occupation, you receive 1 Grain before you pay the costs of the Occupation.

Name: Scrapyard Plow
Card Text: Whenever you use either the ”Plow 1 field” or ”Plow 1 field and/or Sow” Action spaces, you may discard 1 Minor Improvement from your hand to Plow 1 additional field.

Name: Scrounger
Card Text: Scrounger If there are still 9/6/3/1 rounds to play, you receive 4 Wood/3 Clay/2 Stone/1 building resource of your choice. At the end of the game, each player with at least 1 of each building resource receives 3 Bonus points.

Name: Sculptors Son
Card Text: If you have the Joinery/ Pottery/ Basketmaker’s Workshop or their upgrades, you receive an additional 2 Wood/ 2 Clay/ 1 Reed whenever you use a Family member’s action to take only Wood/ Clay/ Reed.

Name: Scythe
Card Text: You may sow fields even if they already contain resources. Place the new resources on top of the existing ones. You cannot sow different resources on a single field.

Name: Seal
Card Text: Whenever you play an Occupation, you receive 1 Clay.

Name: Seal Trainer
Card Text: Whenever you take Fishing, you can put the Food on Seal Trainer. You can still use this food normally. When you take Travelling Players, you can remove 1 food from Seal Trainer to receive 2 Bonus points.

Name: Seasonal Plants
Card Text: Whenever you sow during an even-numbered round, add 1 additional resource of the appropriate type to 1 field you just sowed.

Name: Seasonal Worker
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Day Labourer' Action space, you receive 1 additional grain. From Round 6, you can choose to take 1 vegetable instead.

Name: Seed Master
Card Text: At any time, you may pay 1 food to convert 1 grain in your personal supply to 1 vegetable. At any time, you may convert 1 vegetable in your personal supply to 1 grain.

Name: Seed Seller
Card Text: Whenever you use the Action space 'Take 1 Grain', take 1 additional grain. When you play this card, take 1 grain.

Name: Seed Spreader
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Plough 1 Field” action space, you may immediately afterward sow 1 grain or 1 vegetable.

Name: Self-Employee
Card Text: Immediately before the Round 1 Work phase you may play this card for free without taking an action. It counts as a played Occupation for all purposes.

Name: Self Pasture
Card Text: For each Sheep in your farmyard, this card can hold 1 Wild Boar or 1 Cattle.

Name: Serf
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Sow and bake bread' action, take 1 grain before the action. Alternatively, you can exchange 1 grain for 1 vegetables.

Name: Shared Wall
Card Text: Areas of one or more unused farmyard spaces that are completely surrounded by fences or rooms are now automatically pastures. You may not use them any longer for any other purpose.

Name: Shared Wall
Card Text: Areas of one or more unused farmyard spaces that are completely surrounded by fences or rooms are now automatically pastures. You may not use them any longer for any other purpose.

Name: Shearer
Card Text: Whenever you have at least 1/4/7 Sheep during the Field phase of a Harvest, you receive 1/2/3 Food. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for every 3 Sheep.

Name: Shears
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Day Laborer” Action space, you may receive 1 additional Food for each Sheep on the Sheep Accumulation space up to a maximum of 4 additional Food.

Name: Sheepdog
Card Text: You may keep up to 2 sheep on each unused space in your farmyard. You may not keep any animal other than sheep in your home. The Sheepdog must be fed 1 food each harvest.

Name: Sheep Farmer
Card Text: Whenever you take sheep with one of your people, take an additional sheep from the supply. You can exchange 3 sheep for 1 cattle and 1 wild boar at any time.

Name: Sheep Market
Card Text: At the beginning of each harvest, you may exchange 1 grain or vegetable for 1 sheep. Alternatively, you may exchange 1 sheep for 1 grain or vegetable.

Name: Sheepskin Rug
Card Text: At the end of the game, if you own a Fireplace A1/A2, you receive 2 bonus points.

Name: Sheep Whisperer
Card Text: Add 4, 7, 9 and 11 to the current round and place 1 sheep on each corresponding Round space. At the start of these rounds, take the sheep.

Name: Shepherd
Card Text: During each Harvest, if you have at least 4 sheep during the Breeding phase, take 2 lambs instead of 1 as long as you have room for them.

Name: Shepherd Boy
Card Text: Once you live in a Stone house, place 1 sheep on each remaining Round space. At the start of these rounds, take the sheep.

Name: Shepherd’s Crook
Card Text: Whenever you fence a new pasture that covers at least 4 farmyard spaces, you receive 2 sheep and place them in the new pasture.

Name: Shepherd’s Kitchen
Card Text: At any time, you can convert 1 Sheep to 2 Food.

Name: Shepherd’s Pipe
Card Text: You can hold up to 2 additional sheep on each of the pastures where you keep sheep. In unfenced stables, you can keep up to 2 sheep.

Name: Shipyard
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Fishing” Action space, you can place up to 2 Wood from your supply on this card. At the end of the game, you receive Bonus point for each Wood on this card except for the 1st, 4th, 7th, and 10th.

Name: Shoplifter
Card Text: During the next round, whenever you take building resources from an Action space, you receive I additional Food with each resource.

Name: Shovel
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Day Labourer” action space, you may immediately afterward take the ”Sow” action.

Name: Shovel Worker
Card Text: In the Field phase of each Harvest, you receive 1/2/3/4 Food if you have 2/3/4/5 pastures.

Name: Sickle
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 3 bonus points if you have at least 5 plowed fields, 8 grain and 4 vegetables.

Name: Silo Builder
Card Text: You receive 1 Grain when you build your first stable, 1 Grain when you build your second, 1 Vegetable when you build your third, and 1 Vegetable when you build your fourth. (If you build several stables at once, you may take several resources.) Stone House Builder (tmp5) You may use an Action space that provides both Stone and another resource even if it is occupied by another player. Whenever you build a Stone room, pay 2 Stone less.

Name: Silo Girl
Card Text: Once you live in a Stone house, place 1 Grain on each remaining even Round space and 1 Vegetable on each remaining odd Round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the resources. (If you already live in a Stone house, place the resources immediately.)

Name: Simple Fireplace
Card Text: You can exchange goods for food at any time: Vegetables 2 food; Sheep 1 food; Wild boar 2 food; Cattle 3 food. Whenever you use the 'Bake bread' action, you receive 2 food per grain.

Name: Simple Oven
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Bake bread” Action, you may convert up to 1 Grain into 3 Food with this Oven. When you play this card, you can also take the ”Bake bread” Action.

Name: Singer
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 2 building resources of your choice.

Name: Slapdash Renovation
Card Text: At the end of any turn where you placed a person on a ”Renovation” Action space, you may return that person to your farm. (That person takes another action later in the round.)

Name: Slaughterhouse
Card Text: Whenever another player converts 1 or more animals to food, you receive 1 food from the supply. During the feeding phase of the harvest, you are the last player to take a turn.

Name: Slaughterman
Card Text: Whenever another player converts animals to food, take 1 food from the supply. In the Feeding phase, you are the last player to play.

Name: Sleeping Corner
Card Text: You can use any ”Family Growth” action space, even if another player has already placed a person there.

Name: Sleight of Hand
Card Text: When you play this card, you may return up to 4 building resources in exchange for the same amount of building resources of your choice.

Name: Smallholder
Card Text: Your pastures that can hold 2 animals can hold 3 animals. While you have at most 2 fields, add 1 extra grain or vegetables when you sow.

Name: Smuggler
Card Text: During the Feeding phase of each Harvest, you may convert up to 2 goods as follows: 1 Wood to 1 Grain, or 1 Grain to 1 Stone.

Name: Snack Time
Card Text: At the start of each Harvest, you may choose to skip the Field and Breeding phase and receive 1 Food instead.

Name: Snake Oil Salesman
Card Text: Place 1 food on the next 4 round spaces. At the start of those rounds the last player in turn order receives the food. Once the 4th food is given, you receive 2 clay and 2 stone.

Name: Social Climber
Card Text: Whenever you are the first player to renovate to a clay hut or a stone house, you receive 3 stone. If you are the second, you receive 2 stone; the third, you receive 1 stone.

Name: Sofa
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 4/2 Bonus points if your home has at most 2/3 rooms.

Name: Soil Nutritionist
Card Text: Whenever you plow at least 1 field, you receive 2 Clay. Whenever you sow, you may pay 1 Clay per field to place 1 extra Good from the general supply on them.

Name: South Pole Explorer
Card Text: If you play this card during Stage 1, the next player to use all bottom farmyard spaces recieves 2 Cattle and you receive 1 Vegetable.

Name: Spade
Card Text: Whenever you Sow, you receive 1 Stone.

Name: Spare Fence Posts
Card Text: Once during the game, each player may build 3 fences at no cost when taking a ”Build fences” action. Each other player that does this must pay you 2 Food.

Name: Speculator
Card Text: If you play this card in Stage 1, you may build a Major Improvement for free at the start of Round 9.

Name: Spices
Card Text: Whenever you convert vegetables to food using a Fireplace, Cooking Hearth or Cooking Corner, take 1 additional food.

Name: Spindle
Card Text: Whenever you have 3 (5) sheep during the Field phase of a Harvest, you receive 1 (2) additional Food.

Name: Spinney
Card Text: Whenever another player uses the ”3 Wood” action space, they must give you one of the wood.

Name: Spit Roast
Card Text: Whenever you convert at least 1 animal to food during the feeding phase of the harvest, you receive 1 additional food.

Name: Spring Worker
Card Text: At the start of rounds 5, 8, 10 and 12 you may sow and/or bake.

Name: Stable
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately build 1 stable. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left.

Name: Stable Artificer
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 Wood. At the end of the game, any player who has 4 stables on his/her farm receives 3 Bonus points.

Name: Stable Deliveryman
Card Text: Add 3, 5, 7, and 9 to the current round and place up to 1 of your unbuilt stables on each remaining corresponding Round space. At the start of these rounds you may pay 1 Wood to build that stable immediately. (If you don’t, return that stable to your supply. Until then, you cannot build that stable.)

Name: Stablehand
Card Text: Whenever you build at least 1 fence, you also receive 1 stable which you must build immediately.

Name: Stablemaster
Card Text: In (at most) one of your unfenced stables, you can hold up to 3 animals of the same type.

Name: Stage Star
Card Text: If you play this card in Stage 1, you immediately receive 6 wood. Whenever another player uses the ”Traveling Player” action, you must pay him 1 Wood if you have any in your supply.

Name: Steamer
Card Text: At any time, you can convert pairs of goods to Food as follows: Grain and Vegetable →5 Food; Grain and Sheep →4 Food; Grain and Wild boar →5 Food; Grain and Cattle →6 Food.

Name: Steel Plow
Card Text: Once during the game, when you use either the ”Plow 1 field” or ”Plow 1 field and/or Sow” Action space, you can Plow up to 4 fields instead of 1.

Name: Stockman
Card Text: Take 1 cattle when you build your second stable, 1 wild boar when you build your third and 1 sheep when you build your fourth.

Name: Stone Axe
Card Text: Whenever you use a Wood Accumulation space, you may pay 1 Stone for an additional 3 Wood.

Name: Stone Baron
Card Text: If there are 6/9 rounds to play you receive 1/2 Stone. At the end of the game all players with a Stone House receive 2 Bonus points.

Name: Stone Breaker
Card Text: At any time, you can renovate your Clay hut to a Stone house without using the 'Renovation' Action space. (You must still pay the costs of the renovation.)

Name: Stone Buyer
Card Text: Whenever another player is the first to take stone in a round, you may give him/her 1 food in exchange for 1 of the stone. S/he receives an additional 1 food from the supply.

Name: Stone Carrier
