Card Text: Whenever you take stone with an action, you can also take 1 additional stone. If you also receive other resources, this costs you 1 food.

Name: Stone Cart
Card Text: Place 1 stone on each remaining even-numbered round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the stone.

Name: Stone Carver
Card Text: In each Harvest, the Stone Carver can convert up to 1 stone to 3 food.

Name: Stone Chimney
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each Improvement with a baking symbol you have in front of you.

Name: Stone Crane
Card Text: Whenever another player builds a room or renovates, you may immediately perform this action yourself (without placing a family member). Pay the costs using 1 less building resource of your choice.

Name: Stone Curator
Card Text: Whenever you use a Stone Accumulation space, you receive 1 additional Food. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for every 2 rooms in your Stone house and every 2 Improvements with a printed Stone cost.

Name: Stone Custodian
Card Text: At the end of each Work phase, you receive 1 Food for each Stone Accumulation space with Stone on it.

Name: Stonecutter
Card Text: All Improvements, rooms and renovations cost 1 stone less.

Name: Stonecutter’s Hall
Card Text: In each harvest, you can use the Stonecutter’s Hall to convert at most exactly 1 stone to 3 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 bonus points for 2/4/5 stone.

Name: Stone Deliveryman
Card Text: Place 1 stone on each of the spaces for rounds 8, 10, 12, and 14. At the start of these rounds, you receive the stone.

Name: Stone Exchange
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 2 stone. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Stone Giant
Card Text: Your 1st/2nd/3rd/4th/5th Family member may use an occupied Action space that is orthogonally adjacent to an Accumulation space containing exactly 1/2/3/4/5 Stone.

Name: Stone House Extension
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately extend your stone house by 1 room. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left.

Name: Stone Kitchen
Card Text: At any time, you may convert goods to food as follows: vegetables →4 food; sheep →2 food; wild boar →3 food; cattle →4 food. Whenever you use the ”Bake bread” action, you may convert: grain →3 food.

Name: Stonepiler
Card Text: Whenever you build fences or stables, you may pay some or all of the cost in stone with each stone worth 2 wood.

Name: Stone Sculptor
Card Text: In each Harvest, you may convert up to 1 Stone to 1 Bonus point and 1 Food.

Name: Stone Tongs
Card Text: Whenever you use one of the 'Stone' Action spaces, you receive 1 additional stone.

Name: Stone Trader
Card Text: Whenever you use an action to take stone, you may immediately afterward pay 1 food to play an improvement that has a printed stone cost.

Name: Stone Weir
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Fishing” Action space, you receive at least 4 Food. (If there are 1 to 3 Food are on the space, take Food from the general supply until you total 4 Food.)

Name: Storage Shed
Card Text: Remove 1 built stable and place is on this card (you may not build it again later). Whenever you play a Minor Improvement, take 1 Building resource of your choice from the general supply before paying the costs of the Improvement.

Name: Storehouse Clerk
Card Text: Whenever you have at least 5 stone at the start of a round, you receive 1 extra stone. If you have at least 6 reed, you receive 1 reed. If you have at least 7 clay, you receive 1 clay. If you have at least 8 wood, you receive 1 wood.

Name: Storehouse Keeper
Card Text: Whenever you use a person's action to take reeds and stone, you also receive your choice of 1 clay or 1 grain.

Name: Storehouse Minder
Card Text: When you play this card, you may place 1 to 4 Building resources of different types on it from your supply. At the start of each Harvest, choose up to 2 resource types currently on this card and add 1 of each from the general supply. Each round, you may take all the resources of any 1 type remaining.

Name: Store Owner
Card Text: Store Owner Whenever you build a Major Improvement you receive 1 Bonus point and 1 Food. G8

Name: Story Books
Card Text: At the end of any Work phase in which you have taken a ”Family Growth” action, you may receive 1 Animal of your choice.

Name: Storyteller
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Travelling Players' action on an Action space, you can leave 1 food on the space and take 1 vegetable instead.

Name: Straw
Card Text: Each of your unfenced stables can hold 1 extra animal. Each of your pastures with stables can hold 2 extra animals.

Name: Strawberry Patch
Card Text: Place 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

Name: Straw-Thatched Hut
Card Text: Whenever you add a room to your Clay hut, you can pay 2 Clay, 1 Grain and 1 Food instead of 5 Clay and 2 Reed.

Name: Straw-thatched Roof
Card Text: You no longer need reed when you extend or renovate your home.

Name: Street Musician
Card Text: You receive 1 grain whenever another player takes the ”Traveling Players” action on an action space.

Name: Stroller
Card Text: Pile 6 building resources of your choice, but including at least 1 of each type, on this card in any order. During the Field phase of each Harvest, you receive the top item.

Name: Stud Master
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 1 Animal of every type which is already in your farm.

Name: Sugar Daddy
Card Text: Once you live in a Stone house, whenever you play an Occupation (including this one), you receive 1 Good of your choice afterward.

Name: Sunday School
Card Text: Place a marker on the next round. During that round, you place 1 fewer Family member. At the end of that round, remove the marker and play 2 Occupations for free.

Name: Sunday Worker
Card Text: Once during Rounds 7 and 14, you may pay 1 Food to take one of your peoples’ actions twice. If you use an Accumulation space with that action, you get double the amount of Goods and/or Food.

Name: Sundial
Card Text: At the end of the Work phases in Rounds 7 and 9, you may take a ”Sow” action without placing a family member.

Name: Sun Farmer
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Take 1 Grain” action, you receive 1 additional Sheep. Whenever you use the accumulating ”Sheep” action, you receive 1 additional Grain.

Name: Sunflower Farm
Card Text: At the end of game, if you harvest at least 4 Grain during the final Harvest, you receive 1 Bonus Point per harvested Grain.

Name: Sunrise Admirer
Card Text: At the start of the next 3 rounds, you may pay 2 Food to Plow 1 of the three Eastern-most spaces on your farm (the farm side opposite your home) if they are unused. (Normal plowing rules still apply.) If you choose not to, discard the Sunrise Admirer.

Name: Supervisor
Card Text: At the start of each round, if you have no unused spaces, you receive 1 Guest token which you place that round as you would a Family member.

Name: Supply Exchange
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 1 Reed, 1 Stone, and 1 Food. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Surveyor
Card Text: At the end of each round in which all your Family members were placed on Round 1-14 action spaces you receive 3 different building resources of your choice.

Name: Swan Lake
Card Text: Place 1 food each on the next 5 remaining round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

Name: Swimming Studio
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Fishing” Action space, you may also convert up to 1 Wood to 3 Food.

Name: Swineherd
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Wild boar' Action space, you receive 1 additional wild boar from the supply.

Name: Sycophant
Card Text: Whenever another player uses the 'Take 1 Grain' Action space, s/he must first pay you 1 food. In addition, you receive 1 food from the supply, even when you take the grain yourself.

Name: Syrup Tap
Card Text: Whenever you receive Wood from an Action space, place 1 Food on the next Round space. At the start of this round, you receive the Food.

Name: Tanner
Card Text: Place wild boar and cattle that you convert into food on this card. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 bonus points for 2/4/6 slaughtered wild boar as well as for 2/3/4 cattle.

Name: Tapestry Weaver
Card Text: Whenever you have at least 3 Sheep during the Field phase of a Harvest, place 1 Food from the general supply in your home. You may only place 1 Food per room. You cannot use these Food, but each is worth 1 Bonus point at the end of the game.

Name: Tarzan
Card Text: At the end of the Breeding phase of each Harvest, if you have at least 6 Food in your supply, you receive 1 Animal of your choice (you can’t use it for breeding in the same phase).

Name: Taster
Card Text: Whenever another player is the Starting Player, you can pay him/her 1 food at the start of the round and be the first to place a family member.

Name: Tavern
Card Text: The Tavern is an additional action space. Whenever another player uses it, they receive 3 food. Whenever you use it, you can choose either to take 3 food or to score 2 bonus points.

Name: Tavern Keeper
Card Text: At the start of each Harvest, receive 1 Food and 1 Grain if you have at least 1 room in your home that is not occupied by a Family member. At the end of the game, receive 1 Bonus Point for every empty room.

Name: Tax Collector
Card Text: Whenever another player pays building recources to build a major improvement, you receive 1 resource of those paid (of your choice). You may pay 1 food to the general supply to receive another of the resources paid.

Name: Tea
Card Text: Immediately bring your Family member on the ”Sow and/or Bake” Action space back home. It can take another action this round. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Tenant Farmer
Card Text: You immediately receive a loan of one of each type of animal. Before scoring, return the 3 animals. For each animal that you cannot or do not want to return, deduct 1 point.

Name: Thatcher
Card Text: Pay 1 reeds less to build each room, each renovation, and each of the Water Mill, Half-timbered house, Chicken coop, Holiday home, Mansion and Corn storehouse.

Name: Thatcher’s Helper
Card Text: Pile (from bottom to top) 1 Reed, Wood, Wood, Reed, Wood, Reed. Whenever any player (including you) takes a Family Growth action, you may pay 0/1 Food to the general supply to get the Wood/Reed on top.

Name: The Port Le Havre
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Bake Bread” Action, you may convert 1/2 Clay into 1/2 Stone.

Name: Threshing Board
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Plough 1 Field” or ”Plough Field and Sow” action spaces, you can additionally bake bread.

Name: Threshing Machine Plow
Card Text: Once during the game, when you use the ”Plow 1 field” Action space, you may Plow all 3 middle spaces on your farmyard instead of Plowing 1 field. (The middle 3 spaces are those surrounded by exactly 8 other spaces.)

Name: Thunderbolt
Card Text: Immediately convert each Grain in one of your fields to 2 Wood each, and move them to your supply. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Tide Mill
Card Text: Once in a Round, you may return 1 Grain to the supply and place 3 Food on the next Round space. At the start of this Round, you receive the Food.

Name: Tile Maker
Card Text: If there are 1/3/6/9 rounds left to play you receive 1/2/3/4 Clay. At the end of the game all players with a Clay hut receive 2 Bonus points. (This card does not give Bonus points for a Stone house.)

Name: Timetable
Card Text: Place 1 building resource of each type on this card. These building resources can only be used to pay for Minor Improvements.

Name: Tinsmith
Card Text: You can convert clay into food at any time. You receive 1 food per clay. If any player has built a Well A10, you receive 3 food for each 2 clay instead.

Name: Tower Builder
Card Text: If you are the only player to have only 2 rooms in your home and you use a ”Build rooms” action on an Action space to build at least 1 room, you may build 1 additional room for free.

Name: Town Hall
Card Text: In the Feeding phase of each Harvest, you receive 1/2 Food if you live in a Clay hut/Stone house.

Name: Trailblazer
Card Text: At the start of each round, if you have no empty plowed fields on your farm, you may pay 1 Food to Plow 1 field.

Name: Traveling Salesman
Card Text: Whenever you select a ”minor improvement” action on an action space, you can play a major instead of a minor improvement. If you select a ”major or minor improvement” action on an action space, you can play 2 minor improvements.

Name: Treasure Hunter
Card Text: Immediately place up to 5 Building resources from the general supply on Unused farmyard spaces. The spaces must not be orthogonally adjacent. When you use these spaces, you receive the resource. These spaces count as used for scoring but unused otherwise.

Name: Treasure Map
Card Text: Whenever you play a Minor Improvement, you may pay 1 Food to Plow 1 field afterward.

Name: Tree House
Card Text: Once you no longer live in a Wooden Hut, one of your rooms can hold an additional Family member.

Name: Trees for the Citizens
Card Text: If you have the Joinery or Sawmill, you do not have to pay the Wood cost for this card. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for every 2 Improvements in front of you that have a printed Wood cost.

Name: Trinket Collector
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each of your improvements that doesn’t give Victory points or Bonus points.

Name: Trip to the Lake
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Fishing” Action space, you receive an additional 1 Food and 1 Wood.

Name: Truffle Digger
Card Text: Whenever you use a family member’s action to take wood that is on an accumulating action space and you have 1/2/3 wild boar, you receive 1/2/3 additional food.

Name: Tuileries Garden
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Plow 1 field” Action space, you may immediately afterward take 1 Grain or 1 Vegetable from the general supply and Sow it.

Name: Tunnel
Card Text: You can place exactly 2 people immediately after one another, if at least one of them uses a Stone accumulation space.

Name: Turkey Breeder
Card Text: You may immediately build up to 4 fences and 1 stable for free. At the end of the final Harvest, you must return 4 Wood to the general supply. If you cannot, you must take 1 Begging Card for each Wood you cannot return.

Name: Turner
Card Text: You can use the Turner at any time to convert any number of wood to 1 food each.

Name: Turnip Field
Card Text: When you sow, you can plant vegetables on this card as you would on a field. When you play this card, you can also carry out a sowing action.

Name: Tutor
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each occupation that you play after this one.

Name: Twibil and Twivel
Card Text: After any player, including you, builds at least 1 Wooden room, you receive 1 Food.

Name: Undergardener
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Day Labourer' Action space, also take 1 vegetable.

Name: Underground Shed
Card Text: You may immediately build up to 4 stables for free.

Name: Understudy
Card Text: Whenever an action space is occupied by exactly one of your family members, you may use that action space again with a second family member.

Name: Undisturbed Pond
Card Text: Place 1 Food on each of the next Round spaces, up to the number of completed rounds. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.

Name: Unicycle Driver
Card Text: At the start of each round, you may pay 1 Stone to place 1 of your unused fences as a road between 2 orthogonally adjacent Action spaces. At the end of each Work phase, you may use 1 empty Action space with a road to one occupied by you.

Name: Universal Educator
Card Text: Whenever you play an Occupation (including this one), you receive 1 Food for each of your planted fields.

Name: Unwanted Goods
Card Text: You may immediately discard 1 card from your hand for 1 building resource. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Name: Upholstery
Card Text: Whenever you play an Improvement, you may place 1 Reed from your supply on a room in your home. You may have a maximum of 1 Reed in each room. You cannot use these Reed any more, but each is worth 1 Bonus point at the end of the game.

Name: Vegetable Harvest
Card Text: If you have an Oven, you can convert Vegetables into Food at any time. Take 4 Food for each Vegetable.

Name: Vegetable Importer
Card Text: If you play this card during Stage 1, place 3 Vegetables on the space for Round 11. At the start of this round, you receive the Vegetables.

Name: Vegetable Scoop
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Take 1 Vegetable” action space, you receive 1 additional vegetable.

Name: Vegetable Vendor
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Major or Minor Improvement” Action space, you receive 1 Vegetable. Whenever you use the ”Take 1 Vegetable” Action space, you may play a Major or Minor Improvement by paying its cost.

Name: Vegetarian
Card Text: For each grain or vegetable you convert to food (in any manner), you receive 1 additional food. You may not convert animals to food for the rest of the game.

Name: Veggie Lover
Card Text: In the Feeding phase of each Harvest, you may convert up to 1 pair of 1 Grain and 1 Vegetable to 6 Food. Immediately before scoring, you may exchange 1/2/3 pairs of 1 Grain and 1 Vegetable from your supply for 2/4/6 Bonus points.

Name: Veterinarian
Card Text: When you play this card, place 4 sheep, 3 wild boar and 2 cattle in a container. At the start of each round, draw two animals. If they are the same, keep one. Return the 1 or 2 animals to the container.

Name: Viking Ship
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Fishing” Action space, you can pay 2 Food from the space to the Supply and receive 1 Building resource of your choice and a Guest token that can be used in the following round. G8

Name: Village Druid
Card Text: In the future, whenever you play an Occupation, you receive 1 Sheep afterward.

Name: Village Elder
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 wood. At the end of the game, all players with the most Improvements receive 3 bonus points each.

Name: Village Farmer
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 wood. At the end of the game, all players who have the most plowed fields receive 3 bonus points each.

Name: Village Fool
Card Text: At the start of each round, you may place 1 card face down from your hand under Village Fool. That card can no longer be used for the rest of the game. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus points for each card under Village Fool except the 3rd, 5th, 7th and 9th.

Name: Village School
Card Text: Once per turn, when paying a cost, you may discard 1 card from your hand to pay 1 less Building resource of your choice.

Name: Village Well
Card Text: Place 1 food each on the next 3 remaining round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

Name: Villein
Card Text: Whenever you play an Occupation (including this one), you may take a ”Sow” action afterward. Whenever you play a Minor Improvement, you may take a ”Bake bread” action afterward.

Name: Vinedresser
Card Text: At the start of each round, if you have 2 Grain and 2 Vegetables in your supply or planted, you can pay 1 Wood to Plow 1 field.

Name: Vineyard Labor
Card Text: You may sow Wood and/or Food as if it were Grain. Whenever you sow, you receive 1 Wood and 1 Food before you sow.

Name: Visionary
Card Text: If you play this card in round 1-4, you immediately receive 1 vegetable, 2 wild boars, and 1 stone. Before round 11, you cannot grow your family, unless all other players have more than 2 family members.

Name: Visiting Quarryman
Card Text: Place 2 Guest tokens on this card. Twice during the game, you may place 1 of the Guest tokens as you would an additional Family member. This Guest must use a Clay or Stone Accumulation space.

Name: Walrus Hunter
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Fishing” Action space, you also receive 1 Wild boar.

Name: Wares Salesman
Card Text: Each time any player (including you) builds an improvement or plays an occupation that lets them turn building resources into food, you receive exactly 1 of the corresponding building resource and 1 reed. (If multiple, choose one).

Name: Water Carrier
Card Text: Once any player has built the Well, place 1 food on the remaining Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

Name: Watering Can
Card Text: Immediately add 1 Grain from the supply to each of your planted Grain fields and 1 Vegetable to each of your planted Vegetable fields.

Name: Water Mill
Card Text: After the field phase of a harvest, each player can use the Water Mill to convert up to 1 grain to 3 food. Each player that uses the Water Mill must give you 1 of the food.

Name: Wealthiest Cousin
Card Text: If this is your second Occupation, you get 1 Food for every completed Round of the game up to 4 Food. When you play this card, you receive 1 Grain.

Name: Wealthiest European
Card Text: If this is your first Occupation, immediately take 1 building resource of your choice for each completed round of the game.

Name: Wealthy Man
Card Text: If this is your first Occupation, you immediately receive 1 Bonus point for every Improvement you have. (Improvements played after you play this card do not affect this toral.)

Name: Weaver
Card Text: Whenever you have at least 2 sheep at the start of the Work phase, you receive 1 food.

Name: Weekly Market
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 2 vegetables. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Well Builder
Card Text: For you, the Well is not a Major but a Minor improvement and costs only 1 stone and 1 wood to build.

Name: Well Seller
Card Text: Whenever another player builds the Well, you receive 1 Wood and 3 Stone.

Name: Wet Nurse
Card Text: When building, you may grow your family by up to the number of rooms that you build. This costs 1 food per person.

Name: Whale Oil
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Fishing” Action space, you may place 1 Food from the general supply on this card. Whenever you play an Occupation, you receive Food equal to the number of Food on this card before you pay the cost of the Occupation.

Name: Wheat Farmer
Card Text: Whenever you harvest a Grain field, you may leave the Grain on the field and take 1 Grain from the general supply instead.

Name: Wheedler
Card Text: Whenever you receive food from an action space, you receive 1 additional food. This benefit does not apply to food gained due to an improvement or occupation.

Name: Wheelbarrow Pusher
Card Text: Whenever you use a person’s actions to take exactly 1 resource from a Building resource Accumulation space, you receive an additional 1 Building resource of your choice or 1 Food. In a 4/5 player game, you instead receive an additional 1 Building resource of your choice and 1 Food.

Name: Whistle
Card Text: At the beginning of the Breeding phase of each Harvest, you receive 1 Sheep if you have at least 1 unfenced stable on your farm that is not housing an animal. (You may not convert animals to Food during the Breeding phase of the Harvest).

Name: Wholesale Agent
Card Text: Place 2 each of Wood, Clay, Stone and Reed from the supply on this card. When you have at least 9/7/5/3 Wood/Clay/Stone/ Reed in your supply you receive the corresponding items from this card.

Name: Wild Breeding
Card Text: Immediately add 1 animal of the appropriate type to each Accumulation space containing 2 or more animals. If you have space on your farm, you may take 1 of the added animals. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Wild Fruit
Card Text: Place 3 Food on this card from the general supply. Whenever you receive Grain or Vegetables outside of a Harvest, take 1 Food from this card.

Name: Wild Game
Card Text: You immediately receive 5 Food.

Name: Wild Greens
Card Text: Whenever you Sow, you receive 1 Food for every different type of good that you Sow.

Name: Wildlife Reserve
Card Text: This card can hold up to 1 sheep, 1 wild boar and 1 cattle.

Name: Windmill
Card Text: At any time, you can convert grain to 2 food, without having to bake.

Name: Wine Closet
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 1 Vegetable. Once each round, you may move 1 Vegetable from your supply to a future Round space. If it is at least 5 rounds away, add 1 Vegetable from the general supply. At the start of that round, you must convert each of those Vegetables to 2 Food or 1 Bonus point.

Name: Wintercrafter
Card Text: Between the field and feeding phases of each harvest, you may play a minor improvement or pay 2 food to build a major improvement.

Name: Winter Deadwood
Card Text: When you play this card, you may either pay 1 Wood for 1 Bonus point or receive 1 Wood and pass this card to the player on your left who adds it to his/her hand.

Name: Winter Garden
Card Text: You may sow 1 of your rooms as if it were 1 field. Whenever you have an animal in your home (including House Goat K120), move all resources planted in this room to the general supply. Harvest resources from your room as normal and additionally at the end of rounds 6, 8, 10, and 12.

Name: Winter Pond
Card Text: Place 1 Food on the next 3 Harvests. At the start of these Harvests, you receive the Food.

Name: Wishing Fountain
Card Text: At the start of each round, you may pay 1 Food to receive Building resource of your choice.

Name: Witches’ Dance Ground
Card Text: Place this card on or beside your farm. If you place it beside your farm, you gain 2 extra farmyard spaces (to be scored as normal) and you receive 1 Bonus point. If you place it on your farm, cover two orthogonally adjacent unused spaces which no longer count as part of your farm.

Name: Wolf
Card Text: Pile (from bottom to top) 1 Clay, 1 Wood, andi Grain on this card. Whenever you receive a Good of the same type as the top marker, you may move the marker to your supply to receive 1 Wild boar.

Name: Wood Buyer
Card Text: Whenever a player receives wood from an action, you can buy 1 wood from him/her for 1 food (even without his/her agreement).

Name: Wood Cart
Card Text: Whenever you use a person to take wood that is on an action space, you receive 2 additional wood.

Name: Wood Carver
Card Text: Each round, either an Improvement, a room of a Wooden hut, a stable or a fence costs you 1 wood less.

Name: Wood Collector
Card Text: Place 1 wood on each of the next 5 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the wood.

Name: Woodcutter
Card Text: Take 1 additional wood whenever you use a family member's action to take wood.

Name: Wood Deliveryman
Card Text: Place 1 wood on each space for Rounds 8 to 14. At the start of these rounds, take the wood.

Name: Wood Distributor
Card Text: At the start of the Work phase, you can distribute the wood from the '3 Wood' Action space evenly onto the neighbouring Clay, Reeds and Catch fish spaces.

Name: Wooden Bridge
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”1 Reed” action space, you receive 1 additional food. Whenever you use the ”Day Labourer” action space, you receive 1 additional Reed.

Name: Wooden Crane
Card Text: Whenever you use one of the ”1 Stone” action spaces that are placed in rounds 5-7 and 10-11, you receive 1 additional stone. If you pay 1 food, you can take 2 additional stone instead of 1.

Name: Wooden Hut Builder
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each room in your wooden hut.

Name: Wooden Hut Extension
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately extend your wooden hut by 1 room. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to their hand.

Name: Wooden Path
Card Text: The player with the most valuable street receives 2 bonus points when scoring.

Name: Wooden Strongbox
Card Text: At the end of the game, you get 2 bonus points if your home contains 5 rooms, or 4 bonus points if you have 6 or more rooms.

Name: Wood-fired Oven
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Bake bread” a bread baking action, you can use the Wood-fired Oven to turn any number of grain into 3 food each. When you play this card, you can also take the ”Bake bread” action bake bread immediately.

Name: Wood Gatherers
Card Text: Each round, if you spend 4 or more Wood for Improvements, rooms, stables, or fences, you receive 2 Wood at the end of the round.

Name: Wood Saw
Card Text: Whenever every other player has more Family members than you, you may immediately build 1 or more rooms by paying their cost.

Name: Woodwork
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Build stable(s)” action or the ”Build fences” action on an Action space, you can take both the ”Build stable(s)” and ”Build fences” action.

Name: Wood Worker
Card Text: Whenever you use any Action space, immediately afterward you may place 1 Food from your supply on the space to receive 1 Wood. The next player to use the Action space receives this food.

Name: Workaholic
Card Text: When you play the card, if there are no animals on your farm, place 5 Wood, 4 Clay, and 3 Stone on this card. At any time, if you have 5/7/9 animals, you receive all the Wood/Clay/ Stone on this card.

Name: Working Gloves
Card Text: If you have no Occupation in play when you play this card, you receive 1 Food. Whenever you play an Occupation, you may pay any 1 building resource instead of up to 2 of the required Food.

Name: Worm Bait
Card Text: Whenever you use the ”Plow 1 field” Action space, add 2 Food from the general supply to this card. You receive the Food on this card when you use the ”Fishing” action. Each Harvest, return 1 Food from this card (if any) to the general supply.

Name: Writing Chamber
Card Text: During scoring, you get a number of Bonus points equal to the number of negative points you have, to a maximum of 3 Bonus points. Major Improvements A

Name: Writing Desk
Card Text: Whenever you use an 'Occupation' Action space, you may play 2 occupations one after another. The second occupation costs you 2 food.

Name: Writing Maniac
Card Text: Place 1 Occupation (from your hand) face down on each space for rounds 5,7,9, and 11. At the start of these rounds, you can pay 2 Food to play the Occupation. If you do not, return it to your hand.

Name: Yeoman Farmer
Card Text: At the end of the game, you only lose points in the 'Unused spaces' category and for Begging cards.

Name: Yoke
Card Text: When you play this card, you can immediately plough 1 field for each plough or Harrow I68 that has been played by any player.

Name: Zealous Farmer
Card Text: When you play this card, if you would score at least 3 points in 4 scoring categories (including fenced stables), you immediately receive a ”Family Growth without room” action.

Name: Zoo Director
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for every 3 animals on your farmyard.

Name: Plough Driver
Card Text: Once you have a stone house, you can pay 1 food at the start of each round to plough (at most) 1 field.

Name: Plough Maker
Card Text: Whenever you use either of the 'Plough' or 'Plough Fields and Sow' Action spaces, you can pay 1 food to plough 1 additional field.

Name: Reeds Buyer
Card Text: Whenever reeds are taken for the first time in a round, you may give the player 1 food in exchange for 1 of the reeds. The other player receives an additional 1 food from the supply.

Name: Musician
Card Text: Take 1 grain whenever another player takes the 'Travelling Players' action on an Action space.

Name: Casual Labourer
Card Text: Whenever you receive wood, clay, reeds, stone or grain on an Action space, you can buy one more of the same good for 1 food. An additional vegetables costs 2 food.

Name: Lazy Student
Card Text: Whenever you let another player draw one of your Occupation cards for you to play, you receive 3 food before you pay the costs of the card.

Name: Travelling Salesman
Card Text: Whenever you select the 'Minor Improvement' action on an Action space, you can play a Major instead of a Minor Improvement. If you select the 'Major or Minor Improvement' action, you can play 2 Minor Improvements.

Name: Hand Waterer
Card Text: With the Hand Waterer you can convert clay into food at any time. You receive 1 food per clay. If any player has built a Well, you receive 3 food for every 2 clay.

Name: Store Clerk
Card Text: Whenever you have at least 5 Stone at the start of a round, take 1 extra Stone. With 6 Reeds, take 1 Reeds, with 7 Clay take 1 Clay and with 8 Wood take 1 Wood.

Name: Market Woman
Card Text: Whenever you receive vegetables through a person's action or through a Minor Improvement, take 2 additional grain.

Name: Ploughman
Card Text: Add 4, 7 and 10 to the current round and place 1 field on each corresponding Round space. At the start of these rounds, you can plough that field for 1 food.

Name: Gypsy's Crock
Card Text: Take 1 additional food whenever you convert 2 goods to food at one time using a Fireplace, Cooking Hearth or Cooking Corner.

Name: Wooden Oven
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Bake bread' action, you can use the Wooden Oven to turn any number of grain into 3 food each. When you play this card, you can also take the 'Bake bread' action.

Name: Helpful Neighbours
Card Text: When you play this card, take either 1 stone or 1 reeds. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left.

Name: Vehicular Plough
Card Text: Twice during the game, when you use either the 'Plough Fields' or 'Plough Fields and Sow' action, you can plough 3 fields instead of 1.

Name: Turnwrest Plough
Card Text: Once during the game, when you use either the 'Plough Fields' or 'Plough Fields and Sow' action, you can plough 3 fields instead of 1.

Name: Well
Card Text: Place 1 food each on the next 5 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, take the food.

Name: Fireplace (2 clay)
Card Text: At any time, you may exchange goods for food: Vegetables 2 Food; Sheep 2 Food; Wild boar 2 Food; Cattle 3 Food. Whenever you use the 'Bake bread' action, you receive 3 Food per grain.

Name: Fireplace (3 clay)
Card Text: At any time, you may exchange goods for food: Vegetables 2 Food; Sheep 2 Food; Wild boar 2 Food; Cattle 3 Food. Whenever you use the 'Bake bread' action, you receive 3 Food per grain.

Name: Cooking Hearth (4 clay)
Card Text: At any time, you may exchange goods for food: Vegetables 3 Food; Sheep 2 Food; Wild boar 3 Food; Cattle 4 Food. Whenever you use the 'Bake bread' action, you receive 3 Food per grain.

Name: Cooking Hearth (5 clay)
Card Text: At any time, you may exchange goods for food: Vegetables 3 Food; Sheep 2 Food; Wild boar 3 Food; Cattle 4 Food. Whenever you use the 'Bake bread' action, you receive 3 Food per grain.

Name: Basket Weaver's Workshop
Card Text: In each Harvest, you can use the Basket Weaver's to convert at most 1 reeds to 3 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 bonus points for 2/4/5 reeds.

Name: Clay Oven
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Bake bread' action, you can turn at most 1 grain into 5 food. When you take this card, you can immediately use the 'Bake bread' action.

Name: Stone Oven
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Bake bread' action, you can turn up to 2 grain into 4 food each. When you take this card, you can immediately use the 'Bake bread' action.

Name: Joinery
Card Text: In each Harvest, you can use the Joinery to convert at most 1 wood to 2 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 bonus points for 3/5/7 wood.

Name: Pottery
Card Text: In each Harvest, you can use the Pottery to convert at most 1 clay to 2 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 bonus points for 3/5/7 clay.

Name: Animal Husbandry Worker
Card Text: If there are still 3/6/9 rounds to play, you may immediately take a ”Fences” action and pay 2/3/4 Wood less. At the end of the game, all players with the most pastures receive 2 Bonus points.

Name: Master Fencer
Card Text: Once you live ina Send house, you may pay 2/3 Wood at the start of each round to build 3/4 fences.

Name: Animal Tamer’s Apprentice
Card Text: At the start of each round, you receive 1 Sheep/Wild boar/Cattle for each Wood/ Clay/Stone room in your home that is not occupied by a Family member.

Name: Engineering Apprentice
Card Text: When you play your and, 3rd, sth, and 7th Occupation, you can build 1 stable at no cost afterward. (If you play this card as your 2nd, 3rd, sth, or 7th Occupation, build 1 stable at no cost afterward.)

Name: Riding Plough
Card Text: Twice during the game, when you use either the ”Plough 1 Field” or ”Plough Field and Sow” action space, you can plough 3 fields instead of 1.

Name: Moldboard Plough
Card Text: Twice during the game, when you use the ”Plough 1 Field” action space, you may plough 2 fields instead of 1.

Name: Crooked Plough
Card Text: Once during the game, when you use the ”Plough 1 Field” action space, you can plough 3 fields instead of 1.

Name: Swing Plough
Card Text: Twice during the game, when you use the ”Plough 1 Field” action space, you can plough 3 fields instead of 1.

Name: Fish Farm
Card Text: Whenever another player takes the ”Fishing” action, you can take 1 grain or vegetable from each of your planted fields and place it in your personal supply.

Name: Park Cemetary
Card Text: Place 3 Stone on the ”Plow 1 field” Action space from the general supply. Any player who uses that space may also receive 1 Stone from it. If there is no Stone on that space, you may discard this card and Plow 1 field.

Name: Star Classification Meal
Card Text: Place 1 Food on each remaining Round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.

Name: Stone House Reconstruction
Card Text: You can Renovate your Clay hut to a Stone house at any time without using the ”Renovate” action. (You must still pay for the Renovation.)

Name: Cabbage
Card Text: Place 1 Vegetable on each of the next 2 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Vegetable. Pepper Patch (Cost 1W, tmp2) On this card, you can plant Vegetables as you would on a Field. If you do so, place 1 additional Vegetable on it. (This card does not count as a field when scoring.)

Name: Muddy Bog
Card Text: Place 1 Clay each on the next 2 Round spaces. At the start of each round, you receive the Clay.

Name: Basketmaker’s Workshop
Card Text: In each harvest, you can use the Basketmaker’s Workshop to convert exactly 1 reed to 3 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 bonus points for 2/4/5 reed.

Name: Fireplace
Card Text: At any time, you may convert goods to food as follows: vegetable →2 food; sheep →2 food; wild boar →2 food; cattle →3 food. Whenever you use a bread baking action, you may convert: grain →2 food.
